absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Cross-boundary ministry/API narrative fields and DUR descriptions are not authored by this server and can be influenced by an upstream compromise, poisoned fixture/source, or provider data-integrity failure.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Government/public API data -> LLM instruction hierarchy
candidate_local_attack_path.control: JSON serialization plus prompt text, but no privilege separation
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Fixed endpoints, TLS, server-owned product/card grounding, and the earlier trusted system prompt reduce likelihood and card-field impact.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: upstream data poison -> instruction text in system context -> model follows later instruction -> unsafe ungrounded prose rendered
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: An upstream or fixture/content supply boundary is compromised and the model follows the injected instruction.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Later system-role message consumed by the answer model
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Compromised or poisoned external medical narrative

candidate_local_validation.evidence: External narrative values are serialized into context and the complete string is assigned role=system after the trusted prompt. No content delimiter, lower-privilege role, or downstream semantic validator neutralizes instruction text.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static cross-boundary source-to-role trace; distinguished JSON syntax escaping from LLM instruction isolation.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: No malicious live ministry record was observed. Exploitation requires upstream/fixture compromise or equivalent data poisoning rather than only an ordinary end user.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The primary server system prompt appears first and says the context is data, while card fields receive downstream validation/rebinding; however, a later same-role message is still an instruction-capable channel and general prose is not semantically grounded.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Malicious upstream text can instruct the LLM to alter summary, urgency, or guidance; the general prose gap then allows unsafe medical instructions to reach the browser.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: officialTextKo and DurWarning.describe values are copied into the context at 397-433. Every extra context string is wrapped as a system message at ChatProxyService:206-208. JSON escaping prevents syntax break-out, not instruction interpretation.

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C05] A malicious or compromised government/public API response can control a product/manufacturer/narrative string that DrugContextRetriever treats as official data.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C005] A compromised upstream government record, poisoned fixture/cache value, or other actor able to alter retrieved narrative text controls natural-language strings in DRUG_CONTEXT.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C03] Korean public-API narrative is an external trust domain; user-selected lookup terms influence which record enters context, and an upstream compromise or poisoned record can contain instruction-like text.

[worker-05:R01W05-C009] Cross-boundary ministry/API narrative fields and DUR descriptions are not authored by this server and can be influenced by an upstream compromise, poisoned fixture/source, or provider data-integrity failure.

evidence.control: JSON serialization and a preceding system prompt, but the retrieved blob itself is assigned later system role and no semantic output-grounding rule covers summary/guidance.
evidence.impact_path: malicious upstream field -> DRUG_CONTEXT JSON -> later system message -> model follows embedded instruction -> schema-valid plain harmful summary/guidance -> validator accepts -> user sees it under verified provenance
evidence.preconditions: A trusted upstream or its transport/data pipeline is compromised and the configured model follows the embedded instruction despite the main prompt.
evidence.severity_facts: Exploit reliability is model-dependent and server-grounded card facts limit scope; no code execution or direct provenance forgery is claimed. The remaining sink is primary medical prose, so integrity impact can still be safety-relevant.
evidence.sink: Model-authored summary/guidance returned by ChatProxyController and rendered as care information.
evidence.source: A compromised or malicious public API record containing an embedded natural-language instruction in an official narrative or name field.

evidence.boundary: Government API/cache -> server -> LLM trust boundary
evidence.control: JSON encoding plus prompt-only behavioral instruction
evidence.counterevidence: Ordinary remote users cannot directly write government records, and server grounding prevents the injected text from inventing product identities or provenance on cards.
evidence.impact_path: Instruction-like narrative -> privileged model context -> model follows data as instructions -> unsupported or disclosed user-visible prose passes semantic gap.
evidence.preconditions: An actor can influence a retrieved narrative and the configured model follows the embedded instruction.
evidence.sink: Privileged system-role DRUG_CONTEXT
evidence.source: Compromised external narrative record or poisoned internal cached record

evidence.boundary: External government/public-data response selected through user-influenced retrieval.
evidence.control: JSON encoding, trusted prompt ordering, and structural grounding.
evidence.counterevidence: The text cannot forge a separate JSON message, and server-authored source, directions, warnings, allergy verdict, and product identity remain intact.
evidence.impact_path: Poisoned narrative -> DRUG_CONTEXT system role -> model follows embedded instruction -> semantic output passes nonsemantic validator -> user sees unsafe claim.
evidence.preconditions: An upstream record contains effective prompt text and the provider follows it despite the preceding rules.
evidence.sink: Privileged system message consumed by the LLM, then model-authored rendered prose.
evidence.source: External government/public-data response selected through user-influenced retrieval.

evidence.boundary: Government/public API data -> LLM instruction hierarchy
evidence.control: JSON serialization plus prompt text, but no privilege separation
evidence.counterevidence: Fixed endpoints, TLS, server-owned product/card grounding, and the earlier trusted system prompt reduce likelihood and card-field impact.
evidence.impact_path: upstream data poison -> instruction text in system context -> model follows later instruction -> unsafe ungrounded prose rendered
evidence.preconditions: An upstream or fixture/content supply boundary is compromised and the model follows the injected instruction.
evidence.sink: Later system-role message consumed by the answer model
evidence.source: Compromised or poisoned external medical narrative

evidence.evidence: DrugContextRetriever lines 377-406 copies official narrative text into JSON, ChatProxyService lines 195-208 assigns that JSON system role, and ChatProxyController lines 366-384 preserves summary/guidance. AnswerValidator lines 141-148 only rejects markup/URLs in visible prose. This proves the trust-boundary and sink; model compliance remains variable.
evidence.method: Immutable source-to-system-message-to-response trace at target 654f906e. No live model prompt-injection probe was issued; centralized validation should use a stubbed upstream record and representative configured model.

evidence.evidence: `officialTextKo` values are serialized at retriever lines 397-406 and the resulting string is unconditionally wrapped with `systemMessage(extra)` at service lines 204-208.
evidence.method: Static target-revision trace from API narrative fields through context rendering to system-role insertion.
evidence.proof_gap: No controlled provider run injected instruction-like upstream text; practical obedience depends on the configured model and an attacker gaining influence over provider, fixture, or cache data.

evidence.evidence: officialTextKo fields are copied untouched at lines397-406 and the whole rendered string becomes a system message at ChatProxyService lines206-208.
evidence.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
evidence.proof_gap: No malicious live government record was established; this is a trust-boundary/control candidate, not a claim of current dataset compromise.

evidence.evidence: External narrative values are serialized into context and the complete string is assigned role=system after the trusted prompt. No content delimiter, lower-privilege role, or downstream semantic validator neutralizes instruction text.
evidence.method: Static cross-boundary source-to-role trace; distinguished JSON syntax escaping from LLM instruction isolation.
evidence.proof_gap: No malicious live ministry record was observed. Exploitation requires upstream/fixture compromise or equivalent data poisoning rather than only an ordinary end user.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C05] The primary system prompt precedes DRUG_CONTEXT and instructs the model to use data only, while ObjectMapper produces valid JSON and server grounding owns card fields. Those controls limit structural fact corruption but do not create a trust boundary against instructions inside a later system message or bind plain summary prose to a source.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C005] JSON serialization prevents structural JSON break-out, the base prompt constrains behavior, upstream government data is normally trusted, and deterministic card grounding limits identity/provenance manipulation.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C03] JSON serialization prevents message-boundary breakout, and product/provenance facts are server-grounded, but semantic instruction/data separation is absent.

[worker-05:R01W05-C009] The primary server system prompt appears first and says the context is data, while card fields receive downstream validation/rebinding; however, a later same-role message is still an instruction-capable channel and general prose is not semantically grounded.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C05] A compromised upstream record can steer attacker-chosen, ungrounded medical prose into the primary answer summary or guidance and have it presented under mermAid's government-verified framing.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C005] Indirect prompt injection can steer the model to leak prompt/context content or author unsupported medical prose that the semantic output gate does not detect.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C03] Indirect prompt injection can steer model-authored summary/guidance/translation fields, disclose context, or generate unsafe medical claims that survive structural validation.

[worker-05:R01W05-C009] Malicious upstream text can instruct the LLM to alter summary, urgency, or guidance; the general prose gap then allows unsafe medical instructions to reach the browser.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: Untrusted external narrative is serialized as data but then inserted at system role, allowing it to influence ungrounded model prose. Merge boundary: Low: this is the same source/privilege/sink tuple; downstream semantic-output gaps remain separate.

title: Government narrative is elevated into a privileged system message
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C05] The server elevates retrieved data to a later system-role message. JSON escaping preserves syntax but does not prevent the model from interpreting natural-language instructions embedded in field values, and post-processing does not semantically ground summary or guidance.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C005] Untrusted data is placed in the same privileged system role as server instructions without an explicit non-instruction boundary or typed tool/data channel.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C03] The narrative is serialized verbatim and added as a system-role message immediately after the trusted prompt, while the prompt's explicit ignore rule covers only instructions in user messages and post-validation does not check medical meaning.

[worker-05:R01W05-C009] DrugContextRetriever JSON-escapes external text but ChatProxyService places the whole context in a later role=system message, giving data strings instruction priority instead of treating them as untrusted quoted content.

affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.label: sink

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Any unauthenticated remote caller controls the messages array, its element count, allowed-role string lengths, and array-form content parts in POST /api/v1/chat/completions.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Privileged-role filtering and output limits, with no input count or aggregate size budget.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: oversized body -> full JSON parse -> duplicate/join history -> duplicate sanitized request -> outbound serialization/provider processing -> memory, CPU, worker, or provider-context exhaustion
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The effective HTTP ingress accepts a body large enough to matter; an external limit not represented in the repository could reduce severity but does not remove the application-level amplification.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The route is anonymous and the amplification occurs before the provider can reject context length. Exact heap/latency impact depends on effective ingress size and concurrency.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: JsonNode materialization, StringJoiner safety copy, sanitized ObjectNode copy, outbound JSON serialization, and the LLM request body.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Large anonymous messages arrays, content-part arrays, and content strings in the chat request.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ChatProxyController lines 84-98 accepts raw JsonNode. ChatProxyService lines 136-183 joins all user text, lines 195-227 copies every allowed message, and lines 66-93 serializes the result upstream. No count/length check exists along that path, and the reviewed tests contain none.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable static source-to-sink trace over target 654f906e and full review of the request-construction tests; no oversized request was sent because this discovery assignment is read-only.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Role filtering removes client system/tool/developer roles, but copies all user/assistant content. That is a privilege-shape control, not a size control. A deployment ingress or connector limit may reduce practical size, but no such effective control is established by the target repository.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Oversized histories can consume heap and CPU through repeated copies, occupy workers during serialization and upstream rejection, and inflate provider input-token use or context failures, reducing availability for other users.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The current tracked implementation preserves the exact deferred A004-C02 data flow. MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS limits only model output, and WebClient's response buffer limits only the response; neither bounds inbound JSON or the outbound prompt. No application request-size setting or input-limit exception was found in the reviewed current path.

source_record.title: Anonymous chat requests accept unbounded history before repeated expensive work
variant_titles: Anonymous chat requests accept unbounded history before repeated expensive work
Unbounded message history and text parts amplify one chat request into memory and input-token work
Unauthenticated callers can send unbounded text into a blocking paid LLM extraction call
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The service repeatedly traverses and joins the entire history, reconstructs it for the provider, may perform retrieval plus two provider attempts, and blocks the request thread without a local body/message/input-token limit or rate limiter.

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C01] Any unauthenticated remote caller controls the messages array, its element count, allowed-role string lengths, and array-form content parts in POST /api/v1/chat/completions.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-005] An anonymous caller controls body size, message count, content-part count, and string lengths.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C01] An unauthenticated API caller controls arbitrary message count, text-part count, and aggregate content length in the JSON body.

[worker-05:R01W05-C001] An unauthenticated caller controls the entire JSON body, the number of messages and content parts, and every content string sent to POST /api/v1/chat/completions.

evidence.control: Privileged-role filtering and output limits, with no input count or aggregate size budget.
evidence.impact_path: oversized body -> full JSON parse -> duplicate/join history -> duplicate sanitized request -> outbound serialization/provider processing -> memory, CPU, worker, or provider-context exhaustion
evidence.preconditions: The effective HTTP ingress accepts a body large enough to matter; an external limit not represented in the repository could reduce severity but does not remove the application-level amplification.
evidence.severity_facts: The route is anonymous and the amplification occurs before the provider can reject context length. Exact heap/latency impact depends on effective ingress size and concurrency.
evidence.sink: JsonNode materialization, StringJoiner safety copy, sanitized ObjectNode copy, outbound JSON serialization, and the LLM request body.
evidence.source: Large anonymous messages arrays, content-part arrays, and content strings in the chat request.

evidence.boundary: backend/src/main/java/com/mermaid/chat/ChatProxyController.java
evidence.control: JsonNode binding and message walkers have no repository-defined input byte/count/aggregate limit.
evidence.counterevidence: Frontend histories are ordinary-sized, but attackers need not use the frontend.

preconditions: The endpoint is reachable without a stricter external limit.

evidence.preconditions: Deployment accepts a sufficiently large body or enough concurrent requests.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C01] Role filtering removes client system/tool/developer roles, but copies all user/assistant content. That is a privilege-shape control, not a size control. A deployment ingress or connector limit may reduce practical size, but no such effective control is established by the target repository.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-005] JsonNode binding and message walkers have no repository-defined input byte/count/aggregate limit.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C01] MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, provider timeouts, and extension-entry caps limit response/output amplification, not attacker input.

[worker-05:R01W05-C001] The only nearby bounds are max_tokens for model output and provider timeouts; neither limits bytes already received and materialized by Spring/Jackson.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C01] Oversized histories can consume heap and CPU through repeated copies, occupy workers during serialization and upstream rejection, and inflate provider input-token use or context failures, reducing availability for other users.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-005] One request can consume heap, CPU, provider input tokens, and servlet time before output bounds help.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C01] Anonymous memory, CPU, servlet-thread, upstream-token-cost, and provider-call exhaustion can deny chat service to legitimate users.

[worker-05:R01W05-C001] Large or highly fragmented requests can consume heap and CPU during Jackson parsing, safety concatenation, request reconstruction, and outbound serialization, degrading or crashing the public service before any model timeout helps.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: No body, message-count, part-count, or aggregate-character budget is enforced before parsing, copying, and forwarding chat input. Merge boundary: High boundary: request-size remediation does not close concurrent request admission; the rate/spend cluster is separate even though some broad candidates mention both costs.

affected_locations.detail: The public chat endpoint admits requests without authentication, a caller rate limit, a concurrency semaphore, or an application admission budget in the reviewed chain.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: A normal request performs retrieval and then blocks for the second-pass answer before completing the servlet response.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The normal symptom path can issue two serial LLM POSTs: structured extraction and final completion.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: After paid model extraction, the request can invoke government drug retrieval, adding provider calls and scarce external quota use.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Configured answer and extraction timeouts are 120 and 60 seconds respectively; they bound individual waits but not concurrent admissions or aggregate spend.
affected_locations.label: configuration_boundary

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Any remote unauthenticated caller can submit many concurrent, otherwise ordinary symptom requests that pass deterministic triage.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Only deterministic emergency short-circuiting, per-call timeouts, and output caps; no caller quota, rate limiter, concurrency semaphore, or aggregate work budget.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: request flood -> every request admitted -> slow paid extraction and retrieval -> blocking answer call -> worker/provider/quota exhaustion -> other users cannot obtain care information
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The endpoint is publicly reachable without an effective edge limit and live or hybrid upstream integrations are enabled.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The threat model identifies LLM spend, government quotas, and availability as assets. Exact sustainable concurrency and any deployment-only edge control require centralized validation.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Two serial LLM requests, government API retrieval, and a blocking servlet request per admitted chat call.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Concurrent anonymous POST /api/v1/chat/completions requests containing normal non-emergency symptom text.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Controller lines 84-98 admits anonymous JSON, lines 158-192 runs retrieval and blocks for answer, ChatProxyService lines 115-133 and 66-93 implement the two LLM calls, and DrugContextRetriever lines 155-180 adds government retrieval. No admission component occurs in the chain.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable static call-chain and configuration review at target 654f906e. No concurrency/load request was issued against live or stubbed services in this read-only discovery assignment.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Emergency requests bypass the model, calls have finite timeouts, and response tokens are capped. Those controls reduce individual cost but do not bound concurrent anonymous requests, cumulative upstream spend, servlet occupancy, or shared public-API quotas. An effective external edge rate policy would be counterevidence but is not present in the reviewed repository.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A modest parallel request flood can exhaust servlet workers, consume LLM spend, and deplete rate-limited government API quota, denying medical information to legitimate users.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The current target promotes the exact deferred A004-C01 path to deployed source rows. SearchTermExtractor calls completeJson, DrugContextRetriever then retrieves government data, and the controller blocks on the answer. Individual 60/120-second timeouts and output caps still permit long expensive concurrent work.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Model name and output token count are server-pinned, and each pass has a timeout, but those controls bound one request rather than the number of concurrent or repeated requests.
absorbed_provenance.impact: An attacker can incur provider spend and hold servlet/worker resources for the configured extraction and answer timeouts, exhausting service capacity or the server-owned provider allowance.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The controller calls DrugContextRetriever and then chatProxyService.complete; SearchTermExtractor calls completeJson for pass 1. Repository-wide searches and application configuration contain no rate-limit/filter/concurrency control for this public path.

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C02] Any remote unauthenticated caller can submit many concurrent, otherwise ordinary symptom requests that pass deterministic triage.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C006] Any unauthenticated network client controls request frequency, message count, and message content within whatever generic HTTP limit the deployment happens to impose.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-004] Any remote unauthenticated caller controls repeated chat requests.

[worker-05:R01W05-C002] Any unauthenticated network caller can submit arbitrary chat turns and choose fresh text that avoids useful cache reuse.

[worker-06:W06-CAND-002] Any remote client can call the login-free OpenAI-compatible endpoint with an arbitrary JSON message array and arbitrary text lengths.

evidence.control: Only deterministic emergency short-circuiting, per-call timeouts, and output caps; no caller quota, rate limiter, concurrency semaphore, or aggregate work budget.
evidence.impact_path: request flood -> every request admitted -> slow paid extraction and retrieval -> blocking answer call -> worker/provider/quota exhaustion -> other users cannot obtain care information
evidence.preconditions: The endpoint is publicly reachable without an effective edge limit and live or hybrid upstream integrations are enabled.
evidence.severity_facts: The threat model identifies LLM spend, government quotas, and availability as assets. Exact sustainable concurrency and any deployment-only edge control require centralized validation.
evidence.sink: Two serial LLM requests, government API retrieval, and a blocking servlet request per admitted chat call.
evidence.source: Concurrent anonymous POST /api/v1/chat/completions requests containing normal non-emergency symptom text.

evidence.boundary: `/api/v1/chat/completions`
evidence.control: Generic JSON parsing, output-token cap, and timeouts
evidence.counterevidence: Upstream context windows, server body defaults, and timeouts bound individual requests, but not aggregate anonymous request volume.
evidence.impact_path: Repeated or long requests -> one/two provider calls each -> cost/quota and worker occupancy -> degraded service.
evidence.preconditions: The endpoint is reachable without an external gateway imposing effective rate and body limits.
evidence.sink: Search-term and answer LLM completions
evidence.source: Unauthenticated HTTP client

evidence.evidence: Controller lines 84-98 admits anonymous JSON, lines 158-192 runs retrieval and blocks for answer, ChatProxyService lines 115-133 and 66-93 implement the two LLM calls, and DrugContextRetriever lines 155-180 adds government retrieval. No admission component occurs in the chain.
evidence.method: Immutable static call-chain and configuration review at target 654f906e. No concurrency/load request was issued against live or stubbed services in this read-only discovery assignment.

evidence.evidence: The only `RATE_LIMITED` and `INPUT_TOO_LARGE` occurrences in backend main/config are error-code definitions/messages; no enforcing filter or validator was found, while all conversation messages are forwarded.
evidence.method: Complete target-tree search for request limiting plus static call-count and input-flow trace.
evidence.proof_gap: Deployment-edge limits and provider-side token rejection were not observable in this repository; an integration load test with a mock provider is still needed to quantify impact.

evidence.evidence: No authentication/rate/concurrency gate was found; non-emergency turns can invoke two configured LLM calls.

evidence.proof_gap: The LLM provider or an external gateway may apply quotas not described by the repository; those do not prevent local thread exhaustion and may still charge the owner.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C02] Emergency requests bypass the model, calls have finite timeouts, and response tokens are capped. Those controls reduce individual cost but do not bound concurrent anonymous requests, cumulative upstream spend, servlet occupancy, or shared public-API quotas. An effective external edge rate policy would be counterevidence but is not present in the reviewed repository.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C006] Output is capped with `max_tokens`; provider calls have timeouts, roles are filtered, and platform/proxy limits may exist outside this repository.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-004] Model/output/time bounds apply per call; no caller rate, concurrency, or spend admission is implemented.

[worker-05:R01W05-C002] Model name and output token count are server-pinned, and each pass has a timeout, but those controls bound one request rather than the number of concurrent or repeated requests.

[worker-06:W06-CAND-002] Output is capped at 8192 tokens, extractor results are capped, and provider calls have 60/120 second timeouts. None of those controls bounds input history, request frequency, concurrent requests, or total work per caller.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C02] A modest parallel request flood can exhaust servlet workers, consume LLM spend, and deplete rate-limited government API quota, denying medical information to legitimate users.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C006] An attacker can consume provider quota/cost and tie up request and provider capacity, degrading chat availability for legitimate users.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-004] Attackers can incur provider spend and occupy long-lived request capacity. Attackers can combine model spend with public-API quota and worker exhaustion.

[worker-05:R01W05-C002] An attacker can incur provider spend and hold servlet/worker resources for the configured extraction and answer timeouts, exhausting service capacity or the server-owned provider allowance.

[worker-06:W06-CAND-002] A remote client can drive provider spend, occupy servlet and HTTP-client resources for minutes per request, and degrade or deny chat availability for legitimate medical users.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: Ordinary unauthenticated requests can trigger paid model passes and credentialed retrieval without a caller or aggregate work budget. Merge boundary: High boundary: W06-CAND-002 also mentions unbounded history, but its dominant tuple is synchronous metered-work admission; single-request size remains separate.

title: Anonymous chat lacks aggregate rate, concurrency, and spend admission
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C02] Every admitted request can occupy a servlet worker while performing extraction, government retrieval, and a blocking answer call, with no in-repository per-caller rate limit, concurrency ceiling, or aggregate work budget.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C006] The application has no visible per-client rate limit, conversation-length cap, input-token budget, or bounded message count before one or two paid LLM calls.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-004] A normal request invokes extraction and answer completions under the server bearer key.

[worker-05:R01W05-C002] A normal non-emergency request invokes pass 1 extraction and pass 2 answer generation against the server's LLM credential, with no authentication, per-client quota, concurrency gate, or rate limiter.

[worker-06:W06-CAND-002] The request is parsed in memory, user text is repeatedly joined/copied, pass 1 performs a blocking LLM call and government-API retrieval, and pass 2 performs another blocking LLM call on a servlet request.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Anonymous public HTTP completion endpoint.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Only per-request output, timeout, fan-out, and cache bounds.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Caches can reduce repeated government calls, fixed timeouts eventually release work, and an external ingress may rate-limit. None is an in-repository per-caller or global request budget.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Repeated ordinary prompts -> extraction LLM call -> government retrieval -> final LLM call -> repeat without admission control -> cost/quota exhaustion and service degradation.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The endpoint is Internet reachable without an upstream rate-limiting gateway and the configured provider/key has available credit or quota.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: LLM provider billing/quota, government API quota, and long-lived Spring request work.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Anonymous public HTTP completion endpoint.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ChatProxyController.java:74 always calls answer after a nonmatch. DrugContextRetriever.java:83 calls extraction and line 115 retrieval; ChatProxyController.java:145 performs pass 2. No RateLimit, Bucket4j, or equivalent backend runtime match exists at the pinned commit.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable call-chain inspection plus repository-wide negative search for rate-limit controls.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Deployment ingress or an external LLM account may impose throttles not represented in the repository. Validation should inspect the actual production gateway and measure concurrent/cost behavior without consuming real government quota.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Per-call timeouts, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, retrieval fan-out caps, caching, and emergency short-circuiting bound one request. They do not bound request count or concurrent anonymous callers.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A low-complexity request flood can incur material provider cost, consume the pharmacy/government quota, occupy request threads during long cold calls, and deny care information to legitimate users.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High in the repository deployment surface. The endpoint is intentionally anonymous and each normal request reaches one extraction call plus one final LLM call, with optional multi-call drug retrieval. An immutable git grep found no rate-limit implementation or dependency in backend runtime code.

attacker_controlled_source: [worker-04:W04-S004-C06] Any unauthenticated Internet client can repeatedly POST ordinary non-emergency chat requests.

evidence.boundary: Anonymous public HTTP completion endpoint.
evidence.control: Only per-request output, timeout, fan-out, and cache bounds.
evidence.counterevidence: Caches can reduce repeated government calls, fixed timeouts eventually release work, and an external ingress may rate-limit. None is an in-repository per-caller or global request budget.
evidence.impact_path: Repeated ordinary prompts -> extraction LLM call -> government retrieval -> final LLM call -> repeat without admission control -> cost/quota exhaustion and service degradation.
evidence.preconditions: The endpoint is Internet reachable without an upstream rate-limiting gateway and the configured provider/key has available credit or quota.
evidence.sink: LLM provider billing/quota, government API quota, and long-lived Spring request work.
evidence.source: Anonymous public HTTP completion endpoint.

evidence.evidence: ChatProxyController.java:74 always calls answer after a nonmatch. DrugContextRetriever.java:83 calls extraction and line 115 retrieval; ChatProxyController.java:145 performs pass 2. No RateLimit, Bucket4j, or equivalent backend runtime match exists at the pinned commit.
evidence.method: Immutable call-chain inspection plus repository-wide negative search for rate-limit controls.
evidence.proof_gap: Deployment ingress or an external LLM account may impose throttles not represented in the repository. Validation should inspect the actual production gateway and measure concurrent/cost behavior without consuming real government quota.

closest_control: [worker-04:W04-S004-C06] Per-call timeouts, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, retrieval fan-out caps, caching, and emergency short-circuiting bound one request. They do not bound request count or concurrent anonymous callers.
impact: [worker-04:W04-S004-C06] A low-complexity request flood can incur material provider cost, consume the pharmacy/government quota, occupy request threads during long cold calls, and deny care information to legitimate users.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: Singleton tuple retained: No repository rate limiter, concurrency admission control, API-key requirement, or per-client budget gates the paid LLM calls and scarce government-API operations. Merge boundary: Nested-only: keep separate from any outer-target analogue unless the same deployed instance and control are proven.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: An unauthenticated Internet client can issue many concurrent chat or drug-detail requests and vary symptom-derived search terms or item identifiers to evade cache reuse.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Unauthenticated chat and drug HTTP endpoints.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Local flatMapSequential concurrency and Reactor boundedElastic capacity, not a global admission or quota control.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Caching can absorb repeated identical keys, each single batch is capped at four, and Reactor bounds worker threads. These controls do not cap aggregate unique calls or daily quota; an out-of-repository ingress limit would need validation.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Concurrent unique public requests -> independent Parallel batches -> multiplied uncached upstream calls -> quota/thread/queue exhaustion -> legitimate consultations lose verified data or availability.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Live or hybrid public-API mode is configured, request keys can be varied, and no external gateway rate limit is deployed.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Nested WebClient calls to multiple government drug/DUR operations and the boundedElastic task/connection pool.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Unauthenticated chat and drug HTTP endpoints.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Parallel lines 61-67 create a new Flux per call with only a local concurrency argument. DrugService lines 198, 206, 230, and 241 invoke it repeatedly, while detail lines 137-142 adds three async legs and DurApiClient line 92 adds four more. No shared rate/bulkhead primitive was found in immutable backend source.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Instrument a stubbed upstream and send concurrent unique chat/detail requests to measure peak calls, queue depth, and cache bypass; deployment ingress rate limits outside the repository could materially reduce reachability.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Per-batch concurrency four, WebClient caching/timeouts, and boundedElastic's thread ceiling reduce instantaneous work per invocation, but none caps aggregate requests, unique cache keys, queued tasks, or external daily calls.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A small request burst can multiply into many blocking government API calls, consume scarce data.go.kr daily quota, fill boundedElastic work queues/connections, and deny medicine or facility information to other users.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Parallel's comment claims a burst cannot open excessive sockets, but the implementation has no shared state. DrugService uses repeated and nested batches at concurrency four, and repository-wide detached grep found no RateLimiter, Semaphore, Bulkhead, or equivalent enforcement in backend runtime source.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Any unauthenticated user can submit a chat, open the conversation menu while the request is still working, choose New conversation, and repeat. The menu action remains enabled during streaming.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: An unauthenticated browser action crosses into the public chat endpoint and then a metered external LLM/provider boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: The streaming flag serializes sends only until New conversation resets it; no cancellation handle or server-side admission/rate limit closes the bypass.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: A direct API attacker can already send concurrent requests, and deployment infrastructure may impose undocumented throttling. Browser/network teardown may eventually close connections. No such admission control is present in the immutable repository, and the explicit UI path makes amplification available without custom tooling.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: One browser can accumulate concurrent two-pass RAG requests, consuming paid LLM tokens, government-API quota, servlet threads, outbound connections, and service capacity for up to the configured timeouts. This can raise cost and deny timely answers to users seeking care.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The first request remains pending long enough to select New conversation and submit again. The documented cold path commonly exceeds 100 seconds, making that window practical.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Concurrent blocking RAG/LLM work and its associated external quota, spend, threads, and connections.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Any unauthenticated user can submit a chat, open the conversation menu while the request is still working, choose New conversation, and repeat. The menu action remains enabled during streaming.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: openaiClient lines 45-60 passes cancellation only when a caller supplies a signal. openaiClient.test lines 64-79 expects signal undefined. chatSession lines 311-319 calls streamChat without a signal, while lines 399-418 reset streaming without an abort. ChatScreen lines 441-463 leaves New conversation clickable; ChatProxyController line 180 blocks on the LLM call.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Use deferred mocked streams in a ChatProvider/ChatScreen test: submit once, click New conversation before resolving, submit again, and assert two create calls remain active and the first received no signal. A server integration test with an invocation-counting delayed LLM stub should confirm two concurrent upstream calls and whether client disconnects cancel any blocking work.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The composer blocks a second send inside the same conversation, conversationRef prevents a stale answer from overwriting new UI state, the backend caps output tokens, and LLM calls time out. New conversation bypasses the UI lock, result-discarding is not cancellation, and timeout/max_tokens do not limit the number of concurrent anonymous requests.
absorbed_provenance.impact: One browser can accumulate concurrent two-pass RAG requests, consuming paid LLM tokens, government-API quota, servlet threads, outbound connections, and service capacity for up to the configured timeouts. This can raise cost and deny timely answers to users seeking care.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: ChatScreen lines 441-463 expose New conversation without a streaming disable. chatSession newConversation sets streaming false and only increments conversationRef, which discards a late result but does not stop its work. The old send promise retains the generator, and the subsequent conversation may call streamChat again. The backend synchronously blocks on chatProxyService.complete().block(), while application.yml permits a 120-second answer timeout.

source_record.title: New conversation abandons a costly chat call without cancelling it and immediately permits another
variant_titles: New conversation abandons a costly chat call without cancelling it and immediately permits another
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: streamChat supports an AbortSignal but the production caller invokes it without one. newConversation clears UI state and sets streaming false but retains no AbortController and cannot cancel the old async generator. The next render therefore permits another call while the server still blocks on the previous LLM request; no repository rate/admission control was found.

source_record.closest_control: System/developer/tool roles are dropped and the current user text is screened, but that does not authenticate or safety-scan the retained assistant channel.
source_record.impact: A crafted history can hide emergency or allergy context from deterministic pre-model gates while still steering the model with an apparently authoritative assistant turn, allowing unsafe medicine guidance or delayed emergency escalation.
source_record.plausibility: The two loops have visibly different allowlists: line 155 discards every non-user safety message, while lines 213-222 preserve assistant content. The server has no transcript or signature with which to distinguish genuine prior answers from forged assistant history.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Anonymous user-authored consultation text, including a safety-relevant but ordinary statement such as 'Ibuprofen gives me hives. What can I take for this headache?' without a separately confirmed structured exclusion.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: AllergyDeclaration.presentIn lexical allowlist.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Reaction statement -> matcher false -> no clarification/structured list -> model proposal retained -> product retrieved with no avoided key -> ordinary card reaches the user.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The client has not separately supplied the allergen in the structured extension, and the model/retrieval path yields a product. This is the expected first-declaration state.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The repository classifies allergy controls as safety-critical P0; a false negative is confirmed in target code, while exact product selection varies with model and live data.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: SearchTermExtractor-selected ingredients, DrugService retrieval, then validated/rendered drug cards.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: First-turn free text describing a drug-caused reaction without the three trigger stems.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: AllergyDeclarationTest lines 74-84 already confirms the source reaches false. DrugContextRetriever lines 99-118 and 139-165 deterministically show that false plus empty structured fields bypasses clarification and preserves model-proposed ingredients. The final model/product choice affects the exact harmful card, not whether the safety gate was bypassed.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable target code-and-test trace with a deterministic retriever harness: use 'ibuprofen gives me hives' as all user text, no structured exclusions, and a stub extractor returning naproxen; observe presentIn is false and retrieval proceeds rather than returning AllergyClarification.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Reviewed structured exclusions and unverified-name fields fail closed once present, but neither exists until the server recognizes a declaration and elicits confirmation. The lexical matcher is therefore the closest and sole first-turn control.
absorbed_provenance.impact: The model can select a same-class or otherwise unsuitable alternative and the service can present a retrieved drug card without applying the allergy the user just disclosed, contrary to the fail-closed SA-08 design.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The production target and its test both prove the false negative. DrugContextRetriever relies on this boolean before the extractor and uses no independent reaction classifier. A structured exclusion would protect the user, but the missed first declaration is exactly the point at which the UI has not yet obtained one.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Structured exclude_ingredients and the three declaration keywords fail closed; the product card does not call no_match_found safe.
absorbed_provenance.impact: The service can offer model-selected medicines to a person who just described a reaction, with only name-based allergy comparison and a potentially misleading no_match_found result.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The behavior is executable and deliberately asserted in the pinned tests; the remaining exploitability question is which realistic reaction phrasings lead the model to propose a related or same-class ingredient.

evidence.control: Keyword-only allergy declaration detector.
evidence.counterevidence: Explicit recognized wording or a complete structured exclusion list activates stronger fail-closed controls.
evidence.impact_path: Reaction statement -> detector miss -> model proposal retained -> official product retrieved -> unsuitable medicine shown.
evidence.preconditions: No equivalent structured exclusion accompanies the turn.
evidence.sink: Model-selected ingredient retrieval and rendered drug card.
evidence.source: Unauthenticated consultation text.

evidence.boundary: Pre-model allergy declaration gate
evidence.control: Small keyword allowlist rather than reaction-semantic or structured completeness gate
evidence.counterevidence: Exact ingredient matching can still block the same named ingredient if it independently enters the avoided set; this path concerns the missing declaration signal.
evidence.impact_path: reaction statement without magic keyword -> allergyDeclared=false -> model proposals retained -> potentially related drug shown with misleading no-match state
evidence.preconditions: No structured exclusion accompanies the request and no recognized allergy keyword appears in any user turn.
evidence.sink: Model-proposed ingredient retrieval and rendered medicine cards
evidence.source: Free-text user report of a medicine reaction

evidence.evidence: AllergyDeclarationTest lines 74-84 already confirms the source reaches false. DrugContextRetriever lines 99-118 and 139-165 deterministically show that false plus empty structured fields bypasses clarification and preserves model-proposed ingredients. The final model/product choice affects the exact harmful card, not whether the safety gate was bypassed.
evidence.method: Immutable target code-and-test trace with a deterministic retriever harness: use 'ibuprofen gives me hives' as all user text, no structured exclusions, and a stub extractor returning naproxen; observe presentIn is false and retrieval proceeds rather than returning AllergyClarification.

evidence.evidence: AllergyDeclarationTest lines 80-84 asserts presentIn("ibuprofen gives me hives") is false, while DrugContextRetriever lines 139-165 only suppress model ingredients when declaration state is true.
evidence.method: Pinned source and regression-test review.
evidence.proof_gap: A provider-backed end-to-end test is still needed to measure the downstream medicine selected for representative reaction phrases.

evidence.evidence: Lines 29-31 state the exact miss; the exhaustive pattern list at 38-42 contains no reaction terms.
evidence.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
evidence.proof_gap: Clinical expansion of reaction phrasings requires the repository's mandated reviewer, but the current deterministic bypass does not.

evidence.evidence: The example contains none of the patterns at lines 38-42, so presentIn returns false at 56 and the retrieval query retains proposed ingredients.
evidence.method: Exact keyword-path analysis using the documented `Ibuprofen gives me hives` counterexample.
evidence.proof_gap: A model may happen to avoid unsafe proposals, but the deterministic gate exists because model behavior is not accepted as the safety control.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A028-C01] Reviewed structured exclusions and unverified-name fields fail closed once present, but neither exists until the server recognizes a declaration and elicits confirmation. The lexical matcher is therefore the closest and sole first-turn control.

[worker-02:R01W02-SELF-C001] Structured exclude_ingredients and the three declaration keywords fail closed; the product card does not call no_match_found safe.

[worker-04:W04-P028-C01] Structured exclude_ingredients and explicit allergy/anaphylaxis/intolerance wording fail closed, but natural reaction phrasing has no equivalent control.

[worker-05:R01W05-C006] Structured exclude_ingredients provides a stronger path when the UI has already captured the allergy, but free-text reaction declarations are separately accepted input and can arrive before that interaction.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A028-C01] The model can select a same-class or otherwise unsuitable alternative and the service can present a retrieved drug card without applying the allergy the user just disclosed, contrary to the fail-closed SA-08 design.

[worker-02:R01W02-SELF-C001] The service can offer model-selected medicines to a person who just described a reaction, with only name-based allergy comparison and a potentially misleading no_match_found result.

[worker-04:W04-P028-C01] An allergic user can receive a grounded medicine proposal despite having declared the reaction, including same-class exposure that name-only matching cannot protect against.

[worker-05:R01W05-C006] The model may propose another medicine or related ingredient and the server can render `no_match_found`, despite the fail-closed invariant that declared allergies must discard model suggestions.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: The declaration detector recognizes a few allergy keywords but not explicit causal drug-reaction language, so proposal suppression is skipped. Merge boundary: Low: all members share the same lexical boundary and first-turn safety sink.

title: Reaction-only allergy declarations bypass the fail-closed gate
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A028-C01] The mandatory pre-model allergy gate recognizes labels for allergy but not explicit causal adverse-reaction descriptions, so it authorizes model proposal/retrieval as if no allergy were declared.

[worker-02:R01W02-SELF-C001] The keyword-only declaration rule returns false, so the fail-closed clarification and SA-08 ingredient suppression do not run.

[worker-04:W04-P028-C01] presentIn returns false, so the pre-model allergy declaration gate does not clarify and model-proposed ingredient lookup remains authorized.

[worker-05:R01W05-C006] AllergyDeclaration is a three-pattern keyword gate; a false result allows model-proposed ingredients even though the user's own text reports a reaction.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.label: broken_fallback

affected_locations.label: sink

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A user's plain-language emergency description, concretely 'I am having an anaphylactic reaction'; the safety impact applies to an accidental patient message rather than requiring a malicious caller.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Finite EmergencyTriage RED_FLAGS allowlist lacking the family.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: anaphylaxis text -> emergency matcher misses -> allergy matcher fires -> no resolvable allergen -> routine clarification returned -> no call-119 instruction -> urgent care may be delayed
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The message does not also contain one of the six implemented red-flag phrases. The anaphylaxis path requires no model behavior; other omitted families require downstream failure to escalate.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The control is safety-critical and designed to avoid provider downgrade. The concrete route is deterministic, but clinical expansion of phrase coverage should be reviewed for false positives as the source TODO requires.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Server-authored ROUTINE AllergyClarification for anaphylaxis, or the model-mediated path for other omitted families.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: An anonymous chat message describing an omitted severe emergency family, with anaphylaxis as a deterministic example.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: 'I am having an anaphylactic reaction' matches none of EmergencyTriage lines 36-54, so screen() returns empty at lines 59-67. It matches AllergyDeclaration lines 38-56. DrugContextRetriever lines 139-152 returns AllergyClarification, whose lines 33-52 set ROUTINE urgency and omit 119. Existing triage tests contain no omitted-family case.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable deterministic branch trace using the literal counterexample and full review of EmergencyTriageTest and AllergyDeclarationTest; no test file or runtime state was changed.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The system prompt and AnswerValidator contain downstream emergency rules, but they require the model to run and recognize the emergency. For the anaphylaxis counterexample the model is never called because the allergy clarification returns first; for the other omitted families the model-dependent downgrade is the exact failure pre-model triage is intended to prevent.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A person experiencing anaphylaxis can be told to clarify the allergy instead of receiving the fixed call-119 emergency answer, delaying urgent care. Other omitted families such as seizure and overdose remain dependent on model recognition.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The omission is stated in EmergencyTriage's TODO. AllergyDeclaration's regex and tests confirm that anaphylaxis/anaphylactic are recognized downstream, DrugContextRetriever returns clarification for that state, and AllergyClarification fixes urgency to ROUTINE. Thus the concrete anaphylaxis path does not depend on a provider response.

evidence.impact_path: omitted emergency -> no deterministic triage hit -> model/public API path -> delayed or missing emergency direction
evidence.preconditions: The message does not also contain a covered red-flag phrase.
evidence.sink: Slow two-pass RAG and model-authored urgency
evidence.source: User emergency description in an omitted category

evidence.evidence: 'I am having an anaphylactic reaction' matches none of EmergencyTriage lines 36-54, so screen() returns empty at lines 59-67. It matches AllergyDeclaration lines 38-56. DrugContextRetriever lines 139-152 returns AllergyClarification, whose lines 33-52 set ROUTINE urgency and omit 119. Existing triage tests contain no omitted-family case.
evidence.method: Immutable deterministic branch trace using the literal counterexample and full review of EmergencyTriageTest and AllergyDeclarationTest; no test file or runtime state was changed.

evidence.evidence: `screen("I am having anaphylaxis")` and `screen("I overdosed on pills")` both returned `Optional.empty`.
evidence.method: Executed the compiled target-equivalent `EmergencyTriage` class in JShell with representative omitted phrases.
evidence.proof_gap: Clinical false-positive review is required before expanding patterns; only representative exact phrases were exercised.

evidence.evidence: Lines32-34 list the missing classes and none appear in RED_FLAGS.
evidence.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
evidence.proof_gap: Final clinical phrase vocabulary needs the required second reviewer.

evidence.evidence: No pattern for seizure, overdose/poisoning, head injury with vomiting, or severe allergic reaction exists in RED_FLAGS. A miss proceeds to answer(request) at ChatProxyController:98.
evidence.method: Exhaustive comparison of the source's required-category TODO against every compiled RedFlag entry and its caller.
evidence.proof_gap: Clinical wording and reviewer-approved patterns are intentionally pending; this is a known coverage gap rather than an accidental hidden path.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C04] The system prompt and AnswerValidator contain downstream emergency rules, but they require the model to run and recognize the emergency. For the anaphylaxis counterexample the model is never called because the allergy clarification returns first; for the other omitted families the model-dependent downgrade is the exact failure pre-model triage is intended to prevent.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C004] Six narrow English red-flag families run before any model call, and the model may independently return emergency urgency later.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C05] The model or allergy detector may react conservatively, but neither provides the pre-model server-owned emergency guarantee.

[worker-05:R01W05-C005] AllergyDeclaration recognizes the word anaphylaxis only to suppress proposed ingredients; it does not issue an emergency response, and the model is explicitly not a dependable emergency classifier.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C04] A person experiencing anaphylaxis can be told to clarify the allergy instead of receiving the fixed call-119 emergency answer, delaying urgent care. Other omitted families such as seizure and overdose remain dependent on model recognition.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C004] A person reporting anaphylaxis, overdose, seizure, or similar omitted emergencies can be sent into routine LLM or allergy-clarification processing rather than receiving deterministic call-119 guidance.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C05] No deterministic immediate emergency response is guaranteed for serious presentations the source already recognizes as missing.

[worker-05:R01W05-C005] Those emergencies take the normal slow model path and can receive non-emergency or medication output instead of the deterministic call-119 answer.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: The finite pre-model red-flag catalog omits severe allergy and other documented emergency classes. Merge boundary: Medium: anaphylaxis is a concrete member of the broader omitted-category control; punctuation/whitespace evasion is kept separate.

title: Acknowledged emergency categories bypass deterministic triage
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C04] The mandatory pre-model emergency allowlist returns no match for an expressly documented severe emergency, after which the allergy gate deterministically converts the message into a routine clarification response.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C004] Literal pre-model red-flag matching omits clinically important categories that the source itself records as pending.

[worker-04:W04-P029-C05] The fixed RED_FLAGS list has no pattern for these explicitly acknowledged classes, so screen returns empty and the request reaches the model path.

[worker-05:R01W05-C005] The authoritative pre-model red-flag list omits categories the source itself identifies as required additions.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: INV4 checks subtype presence only; provider shape permits null/arbitrary payload; no server stamping to korea119.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Emergency text misses finite triage -> model returns emergency plus dummy/wrong ShowEmergencyCall -> validator accepts -> controller returns -> browser ignores action -> user receives no visible 119 instruction unless model happened to repeat it in prose.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: EmergencyTriage does not match, the model still sets emergency, and its title/message omit 119. A004-C07 establishes realistic missed families; provider adherence to the prompt is not guaranteed.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The control is intended to guarantee an emergency call and affects immediate physical safety. Exploit manifestation depends on model output, but the supposedly mandatory action is deterministically ineffective in the shipped UI.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen emergency banner and uiActions loop, neither of which renders the nominal call action.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Untrusted model/provider output for an emergency not intercepted by server rules.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: The validator's instanceof-only predicate, nullable provider schema, controller preservation, and frontend type filter form a complete immutable target trace. Existing tests independently demonstrate the gap: backend tests only use korea119(), while the frontend test places 119 in the title and does not render an action.
candidate_local_validation.method: Construct a schema-valid emergency answer with generic title/message and ShowEmergencyCall(new EmergencyPayload(null, null)) or phone 911. Assert AnswerValidator returns no INV4 violation, then feed the serialized answer through the real ChatScreen and assert no 119 text or call control is rendered.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The system prompt asks the model for phone 119 and EmergencyTriage's direct response includes visible 119 text. Neither is a deterministic control for model-path emergencies outside the triage regex set.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A model can correctly classify a situation as emergency yet the user sees a generic banner with no 119 number or call affordance, defeating the safeguard at the moment immediate action matters.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: A constructed ShowEmergencyCall with phone null or 911 passes hasEmergencyCall. ChatScreen has no code path for that subtype. The safe rule-based triage answer independently embeds 119 in visible title/summary, but A004-C07 already proves entire emergency families bypass that rule path and depend on model output.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: EmergencyTriage.java:81-93 emits SHOW_EMERGENCY_CALL twice. ChatScreen.tsx:86-91 renders only text, and lines 113-121 render only OPEN_FACILITY_MAP. MobileShell.tsx:50 requires activeTab !== 'chat' for its tel:119 link. ChatScreen.test.tsx:151-167 asserts only the emergency title, so the missing action is unguarded.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable server-answer-to-active-tab render trace.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Render the real emergency answer through MobileShell on the default chat tab and assert that no tel:119 link exists; product/safety owners should decide whether visible call text is an adequate substitute for the required action.

source_record.closest_control: The banner message and summary explicitly say to call 119, and the fixed footer disclaimer also names 119. The phone number is therefore not hidden, but the structured emergency action and its accessible dialing affordance are lost.
source_record.impact: A user experiencing a recognized emergency loses the one-tap call control the server explicitly required, which can delay contacting emergency services during a time-sensitive event.
source_record.plausibility: Deterministic. The server answer always contains SHOW_EMERGENCY_CALL, the action loop returns null for that type, and the shell link condition is false on the default active chat tab.
source_record.title: The active chat screen drops the server-authored emergency call action
variant_titles: The active chat screen drops the server-authored emergency call action
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: ChatScreen displays the emergency title/message but handles only OPEN_FACILITY_MAP in uiActions and never reads urgency.actions. MobileShell's tel:119 link is deliberately gated to activeTab !== chat, so no call action is rendered while the emergency answer is visible on the active chat tab.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A prompt-influenced, mistaken, or compromised LLM/provider controls urgency.title and urgency.message for an emergency that was not intercepted by the finite server-side EmergencyTriage patterns.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Schema/action presence and markup checks, without server-authored emergency copy or semantic consistency enforcement.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: missed rule emergency -> model returns emergency plus contradictory plain text -> grounding preserves urgency -> validator accepts -> user switches/remains off Chat -> authoritative alert displays harmful instruction.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The server triage regex does not match, the model still selects emergency, and its plain-text title/message are unsafe. Exact manifestation depends on model/provider behavior.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The fixed red styling and 119 link are material mitigation. The candidate is separate from arbitrary dosing and invisible call-action findings because it concerns server trust in the explanatory text of the cross-tab emergency control itself.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: MobileShell's persistent cross-tab error Banner title and description.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Untrusted provider output for a model-path emergency outside EmergencyTriage's finite rules.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ChatProxyController line 372 preserves urgency; AnswerValidator includes the two fields only in forbidden-markup scanning; MobileShell lines 96-99 renders them as Banner title/description. MobileShell.test proves the returned fields are shown across tabs and that the separate fixed 119 link remains. No semantic consistency rule exists.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable model-output-to-cross-tab-render trace with a schema-valid contradictory emergency payload.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The banner has error styling and a fixed tel:119 link, and rule-matched emergencies receive server-authored copy. Those controls reduce impact but do not stop the same alert from telling the user not to act; the model-path title/message remain the dominant explanatory text.
absorbed_provenance.impact: The alert can carry contradictory reassurance or instructions to delay care while visually occupying the application's authoritative emergency surface. A sick user who switched tabs may rely on that text at the moment immediate action is required.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: A schema-valid answer can set level=emergency, include a validator-satisfying call action, and use plain text such as 'You are safe; wait until tomorrow' as title/message. Grounding preserves it, the markup regex does not reject it, and MobileShell displays it verbatim. W01-A004-C07/W01-A029-C04 establish that realistic emergency families can miss deterministic triage and reach this model path.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted LLM output crosses the model-output-to-medical-safety UI boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Pre-model rules server-author recognized emergencies, but the post-model path only enforces emergency action presence and markup absence rather than server-owned emergency copy.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Known red-flag phrases bypass the model entirely, the fixed Call 119 link remains visible, React prevents script execution, and markup is rejected. Those controls reduce but do not remove medical-message integrity impact from ordinary text.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Contradictory or reassuring model text can appear in the application's highest-authority emergency surface and may delay or misdirect urgent care, even though the adjacent fixed link still calls 119.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The request does not trigger a rule-based EmergencyTriage pattern and the upstream answer remains syntactically valid with at least one emergency-call action.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: The global cross-tab Banner role=alert title and description in MobileShell.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: A malicious or prompt-influenced upstream language model controls urgency.level, urgency.title, and urgency.message for any request that does not match the rule-based pre-model emergency patterns.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ChatProxyController.java:366-384 copies answer.urgency unchanged. AnswerValidator.java:101-107 checks only level plus emergency-action presence, while lines 176-181 subject title/message only to a markup pattern. chatSession.tsx:421-423 derives emergencyActive solely from that level, and MobileShell.tsx:96-99 renders the two strings. MobileShell.test.tsx:266-289 supplies response-authored emergency copy and asserts it appears in the alert.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable interprocedural static trace plus inspection of the first-party shell test.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: A controller-level test should submit a schema-valid model answer with an emergency action but contradictory plain text and confirm that the final envelope still carries it; the static branches show no intervening semantic or server-authorship control.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: EmergencyTriage server-authors responses for recognized red-flag phrases; React escapes the strings; AnswerValidator rejects HTML-looking markup and requires an emergency-call action; and MobileShell hard-codes tel:119. None authenticates or replaces the plain-language emergency title and message on model-declared emergency paths.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Contradictory or reassuring model text can appear in the application's highest-authority emergency surface and may delay or misdirect urgent care, even though the adjacent fixed link still calls 119.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High. The immutable pass-through and validation branches are direct, and the first-party MobileShell test demonstrates that arbitrary response title and message strings become the alert contents.

affected_locations.label: untrusted_source_contract

affected_locations.detail: Post-processing validates grounding, actions, sources, and markup but never rejects or rewrites the reserved answerId in a model response.
affected_locations.label: missing_server_reservation

affected_locations.detail: The browser opens the privileged allergy picker whenever the latest parsed answer carries that identifier.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: An untrusted LLM completion, including one influenced by attacker-controlled user text or a government-narrative prompt-injection path, can choose the arbitrary answerId string.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: schema and post-processing accept the reserved identifier as ordinary model output
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: previously failed allergy declarations older than the forged confirmation timestamp are omitted from unanswered_questions, weakening fail-closed allergy completeness on later retrieval
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The model emits the reserved id, the browser receives the normal answer, and the user confirms the displayed picker.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Integrity impact is safety-relevant but requires user interaction and a pre-existing unanswered declaration; the attacker does not directly select the resulting allergy list.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen opens AllergenPicker and confirmAllergies stamps allergiesConfirmedAt
candidate_local_attack_path.source: anonymous POST /api/v1/chat/completions user text or injected retrieved narrative influences the LLM to emit answerId=allergy-clarification

candidate_local_validation.evidence: At target 654f906e, provider schema lines 24-26 allow any answerId; AnswerValidator has no answerId check; openaiClient preserves parsed.answerId; ChatScreen lines 252-255 opens the picker on the reserved literal and lines 327-331 forwards clarificationNeedsSelection; chatSession line 381 stamps allergiesConfirmedAt only when that boolean is true. git grep found the legitimate literal constructor but no server rewrite or reserved-value rejection for model responses.
candidate_local_validation.method: immutable static source-to-state trace plus negative-control search

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The backend constructs the legitimate AllergyClarification object itself and validates many other model fields, but it does not reserve or server-stamp this identifier. React storage and picker guards only preserve the state once the identifier has already been trusted.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A forged clarification can make the browser treat an ordinary model answer as server-authored safety state. If the user confirms it, a prior failed allergy declaration can fall behind allergiesConfirmedAt and disappear from subsequent unanswered_questions, letting retrieval proceed on a structured list that may be incomplete.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The system prompt explicitly tells the model that answerId is any short id, the JSON schema allows every string, and the post-processing validator preserves it. No compromise of the browser or database is required; prompt influence over the normal model response is sufficient, followed by the normal user confirmation action.

React storage and picker guards only preserve the state once the identifier has already been trusted.
source_record.impact: A forged clarification can make the browser treat an ordinary model answer as server-authored safety state. If the user confirms it, a prior failed allergy declaration can fall behind allergiesConfirmedAt and disappear from subsequent unanswered_questions, letting retrieval proceed on a structured list that may be incomplete.
source_record.plausibility: The system prompt explicitly tells the model that answerId is any short id, the JSON schema allows every string, and the post-processing validator preserves it. No compromise of the browser or database is required; prompt influence over the normal model response is sufficient, followed by the normal user confirmation action.
source_record.title: Model-authored answerId can impersonate the server-only allergy clarification
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: A model-owned identifier is used as an authorization signal for a server-only allergy-clarification workflow and for the timestamp that suppresses previously unanswered safety-relevant declarations.
