absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A compromised or malicious LLM provider controls the upstream response body, and an unauthenticated user's prompt can influence the same model output. Either can return a schema-valid drug card whose product, source, and ingredient identity match the retrieved product while changing allergyCheck.status.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Remote chat input and the untrusted LLM-provider response at the model-output trust boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Strict provider schema plus AnswerValidator; both validate shape or product identity, not allergy-verdict equality.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Model-proposed alternatives are suppressed when an allergy is declared, unresolved allergens get a server-authored clarification, and non-user-named blocked products are dropped. Those controls do not cover the intentional user-named-product path, where the card remains and the verdict must be faithfully server-bound.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: User declares ibuprofen allergy and asks about an ibuprofen product -> server retrieves it and computes BLOCKED -> provider returns matching card with no_match_found -> grounding preserves card -> validator passes -> UI shows no-match instead of contains-Ibuprofen.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The user names a product so the blocked product is retained for explanation, allergen binding resolves an authoritative avoided key, and the provider or prompt-influenced model changes only the allergy verdict while preserving product/source/ingredient identity.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: AllergyBadge renders the provider-selected status on a grounded user-named drug card.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Remote chat input and the untrusted LLM-provider response at the model-output trust boundary.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Immutable schema lines 227-254 expose the verdict to provider authorship. DrugContextRetriever.java lines 226-230 contains the trusted verdict but lines 350-354 omit it from GroundedDrug; ChatProxyController.java lines 207-221 preserves model drugs; AnswerValidator.java lines 80-87 and 123-139 never compare allergy status; AllergyBadge.tsx lines 14-36 renders the downgraded state.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Central validation should execute that end-to-end regression and confirm the production ObjectMapper/provider response path. No repository evidence shows another post-controller response transformer that restores the server verdict.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: AnswerValidator is the closest post-provider control, but INV2 is one-directional: it checks only that a model claiming BLOCKED names a match. The prompt's copy instruction and strict JSON schema constrain shape, not truth, and the frontend's cautious no-match wording cannot recover the lost server verdict.
absorbed_provenance.impact: For the supported path where an allergic user asks about a named medicine that contains the avoided ingredient, a server-computed BLOCKED verdict can reach the browser as no_match_found. The user loses the explicit contains-allergen warning and may take a medicine the server had already identified as matching the declared allergy.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The system prompt itself tells the model to copy allergyCheck from DRUG_CONTEXT, proving the server value exists but is enforced only by instruction. DrugService keeps a blocked user-named product; DrugContextRetriever renders the blocked verdict; GroundedDrug omits it; ground() retains the model card; and AnswerValidator has no inverse or equality check for no_match_found. A downgraded card with the correct product/source/ingredient set therefore satisfies every implemented invariant.

source_record.title: Provider-authored allergy verdict can downgrade a server BLOCKED match
variant_titles: Provider-authored allergy verdict can downgrade a server BLOCKED match
A model can downgrade a server-blocked allergy verdict to no_match_found
Model output can downgrade a server-computed blocked allergy verdict
Model output can downgrade a server-computed BLOCKED allergy verdict

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-04:W04-S002-C02] A compromised or malicious LLM provider controls the upstream response body, and an unauthenticated user's prompt can influence the same model output. Either can return a schema-valid drug card whose product, source, and ingredient identity match the retrieved product while changing allergyCheck.status.

[worker-05:R01W05-C010] The pass-2 model authors each nested-revision drug card, including allergyCheck, after receiving the server-computed verdict only as prompt context.

evidence.boundary: Remote chat input and the untrusted LLM-provider response at the model-output trust boundary.
evidence.control: Strict provider schema plus AnswerValidator; both validate shape or product identity, not allergy-verdict equality.
evidence.counterevidence: Model-proposed alternatives are suppressed when an allergy is declared, unresolved allergens get a server-authored clarification, and non-user-named blocked products are dropped. Those controls do not cover the intentional user-named-product path, where the card remains and the verdict must be faithfully server-bound.
evidence.impact_path: User declares ibuprofen allergy and asks about an ibuprofen product -> server retrieves it and computes BLOCKED -> provider returns matching card with no_match_found -> grounding preserves card -> validator passes -> UI shows no-match instead of contains-Ibuprofen.
evidence.preconditions: The user names a product so the blocked product is retained for explanation, allergen binding resolves an authoritative avoided key, and the provider or prompt-influenced model changes only the allergy verdict while preserving product/source/ingredient identity.
evidence.sink: AllergyBadge renders the provider-selected status on a grounded user-named drug card.
evidence.source: Remote chat input and the untrusted LLM-provider response at the model-output trust boundary.

evidence.boundary: External model to server-computed allergy verdict
evidence.control: No equality/rebinding check for allergyCheck
evidence.counterevidence: Outer target code fixes this class by rebuilding card fields from GroundedDrug; the nested tree is absent from the outer target commit.
evidence.impact_path: server computes BLOCKED -> prompt tells model -> model downgrades -> validator checks unrelated card fields -> unsafe state rendered
evidence.preconditions: The nested be68 backend/frontend is executed and the model deviates while keeping the card otherwise grounded.
evidence.sink: Nested AllergyBadge rendering
evidence.source: Prompt-influenced model drug card

evidence.evidence: Immutable schema lines 227-254 expose the verdict to provider authorship. DrugContextRetriever.java lines 226-230 contains the trusted verdict but lines 350-354 omit it from GroundedDrug; ChatProxyController.java lines 207-221 preserves model drugs; AnswerValidator.java lines 80-87 and 123-139 never compare allergy status; AllergyBadge.tsx lines 14-36 renders the downgraded state.
evidence.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
evidence.proof_gap: Central validation should execute that end-to-end regression and confirm the production ObjectMapper/provider response path. No repository evidence shows another post-controller response transformer that restores the server verdict.

evidence.evidence: Nested INV2 applies only when the model itself says BLOCKED; INV6 compares no allergy field. ground() preserves the model drug list, so the downgraded card reaches the renderer.
evidence.method: Constructive static validator trace using the correct retrieved product/source/ingredients with model allergyCheck=no_match_found despite server status=blocked.
evidence.proof_gap: The exact nested path is absent from outer revision 654f906e00e81648d1482210b6a9171747dddd75. Reachability depends on the immutable nested revision be68c887fe118d02d2bfac1987a0c51aa62abbb6 being run or promoted.

closest_control:
[worker-04:W04-S002-C02] AnswerValidator is the closest post-provider control, but INV2 is one-directional: it checks only that a model claiming BLOCKED names a match. The prompt's copy instruction and strict JSON schema constrain shape, not truth, and the frontend's cautious no-match wording cannot recover the lost server verdict.

[worker-05:R01W05-C010] The nested validator binds product identity/source/ingredient set, but those equalities do not imply the model copied the allergy verdict. The outer target counterpart now rebinds card safety fields, which is counterevidence for current deployed outer code.
impact:
[worker-04:W04-S002-C02] For the supported path where an allergic user asks about a named medicine that contains the avoided ingredient, a server-computed BLOCKED verdict can reach the browser as no_match_found. The user loses the explicit contains-allergen warning and may take a medicine the server had already identified as matching the declared allergy.

[worker-05:R01W05-C010] A model can change a BLOCKED medicine to no_match_found or unknown and the nested UI will render the downgraded state, directly defeating the allergy safety boundary.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: The nested grounder preserves model-authored allergyCheck and the validator never compares it with the server verdict. Merge boundary: Low within the same nested revision; the current outer target reportedly rebinds this field.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A schema-less or schema-ignoring LLM response containing an otherwise grounded drug card with allergyCheck:null.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted provider JSON crosses server deserialization and the browser's unchecked TypeScript assertion boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Every nested field the browser dereferences should be validated or normalized before the answer is declared safe.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Schema-enforced compliant providers block this shape, and the canonical disclaimer lives outside the answer. The configured compatibility/retry behavior makes schema enforcement non-universal, and no React error boundary contains the crash.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: One malformed provider answer throws during React rendering and can blank the chat application for a sick user instead of showing the server-authored refusal and safety information.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Structured output is unavailable, rejected, or ignored, and the provider emits a grounded card with a null allergyCheck.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: AllergyBadge's unconditional check.status dereference during the answer render.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: A schema-less or schema-ignoring LLM response containing an otherwise grounded drug card with allergyCheck:null.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: StructuredOutputFallback.java:52-56 counts null list elements only; withGuarantees preserves drugs. AnswerValidator.java:83 checks `check != null` and lines 195-197 skip absent allergyCheck. openaiClient.ts:140 preserves parsed.drugs, then AllergyBadge.tsx:15 reads check.status with no error boundary present in frontend/src.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable schema-less parse, validator branch, and React dereference trace.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Run a mocked controller/browser test returning a valid grounded card with allergyCheck:null to capture the empty server violation list and React exception.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Strict response schema disallows null allergyCheck when supported, and StructuredOutputFallback counts null elements in drugs/guidance/actions. The product explicitly supports models and retries where that schema is absent, and the fallback omits nested-field validation.
absorbed_provenance.impact: One malformed provider answer throws during React rendering and can blank the chat application for a sick user instead of showing the server-authored refusal and safety information.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High on the supported no-schema path. LlmProperties permits models outside the structured-output allowlist and ChatProxyService retries a schema 400 without response_format, while Jackson accepts null record components.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted provider JSON crosses both server and browser structural-validation boundaries.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Nested arrays dereferenced by the browser should be non-null by construction or cause a fail-closed answer.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Schema-compliant providers and honest model output produce arrays. The product's documented compatibility and retry behavior means those are not universal hard controls.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: A malformed provider response accepted as validated can throw during React rendering and deny the complete chat answer and its medical-safety context to the user.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The response is accepted without an effective strict schema and otherwise contains a grounded, valid drug card.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen's unconditional drug.warnings.map call.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: A schema-less or schema-ignoring LLM response containing an otherwise grounded drug card with warnings:null.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: StructuredOutputFallback.java:52-56 never counts nested warnings; AnswerValidator.java:194 calls nullSafe and lines 202-204 convert null to an empty iterable; openaiClient.ts:140 preserves parsed.drugs; ChatScreen.tsx:103 calls map directly on drug.warnings.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable schema-less parse, validator helper, frontend pass-through, and render-sink trace.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Run a mocked controller/browser test with warnings:null to capture the empty validator result and render exception independently of W04-S003-C07.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The strict schema requires an array and StructuredOutputFallback fills null top-level lists, but neither applies an equivalent nested guarantee when response_format is unavailable or ignored.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A malformed provider response accepted as validated can throw during React rendering and deny the complete chat answer and its medical-safety context to the user.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High on the explicitly supported schema-less/retry path. The null value is accepted by Jackson records, no server invariant requires a list, and TypeScript types do not validate runtime JSON.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted structured LLM output.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Null/blank defaulting to a constant server disclaimer.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Null, blank, malformed, and local-fallback answers receive the canonical disclaimer. The bypass is limited to structurally valid nonblank model text.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Model emits nonblank misleading disclaimer -> fallback considers it present -> ground preserves it -> validator sees no markup -> browser receives misleading safety copy.

candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Rendered disclaimer field in the returned answer.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: StructuredOutputFallback.java:94 selects r.disclaimer for every nonblank value; ChatProxyController.java:221 retains it; AnswerValidator.java:182-184 and 141-145 apply only the markup regex.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Malicious or mistaken LLM response field at the model-output boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Strict enum shape plus a prompt instruction carrying the real value.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: The product and ingredient identities remain authentic, and a cooperative model usually copies the field; those controls do not detect the exact misclassification.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Government record says prescription -> prompt contains prescription -> model returns otc with otherwise grounded tuple -> validator passes -> user sees incorrect OTC status.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: At least one retrieved prescription product and a schema-valid provider response that changes the enum.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Serialized drug card returned to the browser.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Malicious or mistaken LLM response field at the model-output boundary.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: DrugContextRetriever.java:190 writes the official status into prompt JSON; its GroundedDrug record at 351 contains no status. ChatProxyController.java:217 retains the card and AnswerValidator.java:123-139 compares only Korean name, source, and ingredient keys.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable field-by-field grounding/validator trace.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Add a controller test with a grounded prescription Drug and a model card marked otc; confirm the response is accepted unchanged.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The model prompt carries the correct enum and strict output schema limits values to prescription, otc, or unknown. It does not bind the chosen value to the retrieved record.
absorbed_provenance.impact: An English-speaking user can be told that a prescription-only medicine is OTC under official provenance, changing how they interpret access and use of the product.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High. Returning the correct Korean product name, source id, and ingredients with prescriptionStatus='otc' satisfies every implemented invariant even when the retrieved Drug says prescription.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: An origin-running script, browser extension, person with device access, or stale build can place an otherwise well-shaped ChatSession in sessionStorage whose assistant `content` is JSON-valid but violates MermAidAnswer's runtime structure. The current backend's validated live-response path is exact counterevidence for anonymous model-only injection.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: JSON-valid assistant content inside untrusted sessionStorage.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: JSON syntax check plus default-filling coercion, with no runtime answer schema or nested-array element validator.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Syntactically invalid answer text is caught and rendered with no drug cards, and server-delivered live answers are validated before emission. The candidate is intentionally limited to valid-JSON, wrong-shape storage restoration.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Storage writer creates well-shaped session/messages but malformed answer JSON -> restore JSON.parse succeeds -> parseAnswer preserves wrong nested value -> restored turn enters state -> ChatScreen dereferences it -> whole root render aborts.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The actor or stale build can write the tab's sessionStorage and the stored wrapper/messages retain the expected outer roles/string fields; the user reloads before clearing/closing the tab.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen drug/action rendering under an application root without an error boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: JSON-valid assistant content inside untrusted sessionStorage.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: At be68c887fe118d02d2bfac1987a0c51aa62abbb6, chatSession.tsx lines 47-60 accepts any JSON string and stores parseAnswer's result; openaiClient.ts lines 114-145 preserves a truthy `drugs` array without element validation; ChatScreen.tsx lines 97-100 dereferences every element; main.tsx has no error boundary.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: A focused restore test should store a valid ChatSession with assistant content `{"summary":"x","drugs":[null]}`, mount the app, and confirm parseAnswer accepts it and ChatScreen currently throws instead of dropping the turn.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The backend buffers, deserializes, grounds, and validates current live answers, and parseAnswer safely removes drug cards from syntactically invalid/truncated prose. The storage restore path bypasses the backend and only checks syntax, so neither control rejects JSON-valid wrong nested types.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A crafted or stale stored assistant turn can crash the uncontained React tree on every reload of that tab, hiding the disclaimer, emergency UI, and all care-navigation screens until the session is cleared.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High for deterministic malformed-storage behavior and limited by the browser-storage write precondition. The payload passes the wrapper check, message-string check, explicit JSON.parse, and withGuarantees because summary is nonempty; the later drug map then dereferences null.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A browser extension, local same-device actor, XSS, or compromised top-level third-party script able to write the origin's sessionStorage. W01-A022-C01 already records the Naver SDK's ambient storage capability; this candidate covers the distinct persisted medical-integrity sink.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Tab-scoped storage plus version/shape parsing, without authenticity or a safe untrusted-storage rendering mode.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Compromised extension/third-party script/local actor -> writes parseable assistant JSON -> reload -> restoreSession treats it as validated -> forged live medical guidance and provenance render.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The victim's tab remains open or is reloaded with the stored entry, and an actor already has browser-local or same-origin script write authority. Ordinary cross-origin web content cannot write the key.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The sink affects core medical-output integrity, but exploitability depends on a browser-side or local foothold that can also manipulate the page directly; severity should account for the persistence benefit rather than treating it as a fresh remote XSS.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen's normal medical answer and DrugCard rendering path.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Write access to same-origin sessionStorage.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: The immutable trace is complete: loadChatSession returns same-version data, restoreSession accepts any JSON assistant string, parseAnswer preserves attacker-supplied medical arrays/fields, and AnsweredTurn renders them. Existing corruption tests cover stale versions/non-JSON and availability, not answer authenticity. No live third-party compromise was attempted.
candidate_local_validation.method: Add a browser test that writes a same-version chatSession.v2 assistant JSON with dataStatus live, a forged summary/drug/sourceRef and a valid disclaimer, mounts ChatProvider, and observes whether the forged card and provenance render after reload.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: sessionStorage limits lifetime to the tab; schema-version checks, JSON.parse and parseAnswer prevent some crashes and fill missing fields. None proves that a complete supplied answer came from the backend or preserves server-owned provenance.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A crafted stored blob can survive a reload and present attacker-chosen treatment prose, drug cards, allergy state and provenance as an ordinary verified answer, potentially influencing medication decisions.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: storage.ts itself documents Web Storage as untrusted and validates only the wrapper version. The current key bump prevents restoration of one obsolete schema but does not authenticate v2 records. React escaping prevents HTML injection but not false medical content. The attack requires browser-side/local write authority and therefore does not give an ordinary remote HTTP user a new path.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: parseAnswer fills defaults but accepts supplied summary, urgency, drugs, sourceRefs, warnings and dataStatus as if the backend had already validated them.
affected_locations.label: root_control

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: A browser extension, local same-device actor, XSS, or compromised top-level third-party script able to write the origin's sessionStorage. W01-A022-C01 already records the Naver SDK's ambient storage capability; this candidate covers the distinct persisted medical-integrity sink.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Tab-scoped storage plus version/shape parsing, without authenticity or a safe untrusted-storage rendering mode.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Compromised extension/third-party script/local actor -> writes parseable assistant JSON -> reload -> restoreSession treats it as validated -> forged live medical guidance and provenance render.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The victim's tab remains open or is reloaded with the stored entry, and an actor already has browser-local or same-origin script write authority. Ordinary cross-origin web content cannot write the key.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The sink affects core medical-output integrity, but exploitability depends on a browser-side or local foothold that can also manipulate the page directly; severity should account for the persistence benefit rather than treating it as a fresh remote XSS.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: ChatScreen's normal medical answer and DrugCard rendering path.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Write access to same-origin sessionStorage.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: The immutable trace is complete: loadChatSession returns same-version data, restoreSession accepts any JSON assistant string, parseAnswer preserves attacker-supplied medical arrays/fields, and AnsweredTurn renders them. Existing corruption tests cover stale versions/non-JSON and availability, not answer authenticity. No live third-party compromise was attempted.
candidate_local_validation.method: Add a browser test that writes a same-version chatSession.v2 assistant JSON with dataStatus live, a forged summary/drug/sourceRef and a valid disclaimer, mounts ChatProvider, and observes whether the forged card and provenance render after reload.

source_record.closest_control: sessionStorage limits lifetime to the tab; schema-version checks, JSON.parse and parseAnswer prevent some crashes and fill missing fields. None proves that a complete supplied answer came from the backend or preserves server-owned provenance.
source_record.impact: A crafted stored blob can survive a reload and present attacker-chosen treatment prose, drug cards, allergy state and provenance as an ordinary verified answer, potentially influencing medication decisions.
source_record.plausibility: storage.ts itself documents Web Storage as untrusted and validates only the wrapper version. The current key bump prevents restoration of one obsolete schema but does not authenticate v2 records. React escaping prevents HTML injection but not false medical content. The attack requires browser-side/local write authority and therefore does not give an ordinary remote HTTP user a new path.
source_record.title: Browser-session tampering can restore forged medical answers as server-validated
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The restore path equates parseable same-version browser storage with a server-validated answer and supplies no authenticity, server provenance revalidation, or safe display-only downgrade.

affected_locations.detail: The page origin holds a persistent deviceId, consultation messages/session allergies in sessionStorage, and opt-in allergy preferences in localStorage.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: Opening a map appends a third-party script element directly to document.head; it is not isolated in a sandboxed/cross-origin iframe.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: The fetched SDK runs in the application document and receives normal page JavaScript authority before the map is marked ready.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Chat and Map remain mounted in the same top-level document and the SDK loads when the Map tab becomes active.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A compromised/malicious Naver Maps response, CDN/DNS/TLS supply-chain path, or vendor-side script change; ordinary remote application users cannot select the script authority.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Fixed HTTPS vendor host and intentional provider trust; no capability isolation once loaded.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Vendor/CDN compromise -> malicious maps.js executes in app origin -> reads consultation/device/allergy state -> exfiltrates -> privacy and anonymous-profile capability compromise.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: User opens Map, sensitive same-tab/storage state exists, and the trusted vendor delivery path is compromised or behaves maliciously. A normal application attacker cannot satisfy this directly.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: Potential data is health-adjacent and the deviceId is a bearer-like selector, but exploitability depends on a third-party supply-chain compromise and no current malicious SDK behavior was observed.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Same-origin sessionStorage/localStorage/DOM and outbound network APIs available to the SDK.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Compromised third-party map script response after the user activates Map.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: useNaverMap lines 69-85 creates an external script in the top-level document. storage.ts lines 46-84 and 108-129 places the bearer ID, chat/allergy session, and opted-in preferences in the same origin's Web Storage. Standard browser execution gives loaded scripts the document's origin. No iframe/sandbox/SRI boundary exists in index.html or the hook.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable browser trust-boundary trace; no Naver response was intercepted and no data was exfiltrated.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The script host and scheme are hard-coded, the key is public by design, loading occurs only when a map is used, and visible auth failures are handled. None scopes the loaded code's access after execution.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A compromised SDK response could read and exfiltrate current-tab symptom/answer transcripts, opt-in allergy preferences, saved places, and the persistent deviceId capability used for anonymous profile access.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Browser security grants external scripts the embedding document's origin, independent of the script URL's host. The sensitive storage is explicit. The authority is fixed HTTPS and Naver is an intentional trusted provider, so this is a supply-chain/architecture risk rather than evidence of current malicious behavior.

affected_locations.detail: Anonymous POST /api/v1/profiles/{deviceId}/allergies accepts deviceId directly from the path.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The consent-off branch concatenates the request deviceId into the ApiException internal message.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: ApiException passes the internal message to RuntimeException unchanged.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: New profiles deterministically begin with rememberAllergies=false, making the error branch reachable without setup.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: The handler writes e.getMessage(), including deviceId, to the application warning log.
affected_locations.label: sink

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: The deviceId path segment on the anonymous POST /api/v1/profiles/{deviceId}/allergies endpoint; ordinary clients also send their real bearer-like identifier here.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: The handler sanitizes the HTTP response but has no sensitive-field classification or redaction before logging the internal exception message.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Path bearer ID -> ProfileService exception message -> RuntimeException.getMessage -> application log -> log reader can reuse the ID against anonymous profile CRUD.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The profile has rememberAllergies=false (the default), the request body passes validation, and an unintended party later gains access to application or aggregated logs.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The value is a bearer capability rather than a password and profile data is intentionally limited, but possession permits anonymous read/write of allergy and saved-place state. The issue is server-runtime logging, not merely a nested developer-worktree artifact.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: SLF4J warning log at GlobalExceptionHandler.handled line 38.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: A legitimate or attacker-influenced anonymous allergy-create request carrying a profile deviceId.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ProfileController lines 48-53 bind deviceId from the anonymous path. ProfileService lines 66-72 call getOrCreate, observe default-off consent, and build the message 'profile ' + deviceId + ' has not opted in'. ApiException line 27 supplies that string to RuntimeException. GlobalExceptionHandler line 38 logs e.getMessage() with SLF4J. UserProfile lines 41-48 confirm the default-off state.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static source-to-sink trace over immutable be68c887 blobs that are byte-identical to the outer 654f906e target counterparts; no later centralized validation phase or live request was run.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: ApiException documents its message as internal and GlobalExceptionHandler replaces it with a safe browser sentence, but there is no equivalent log redaction; line 38 logs the sensitive internal message itself.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Application logs retain a capability that is sufficient to read and mutate the corresponding anonymous profile, exposing or altering saved allergies, consent state, country, favorites, aliases, and memos to anyone who later obtains those logs.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The threat model explicitly treats deviceId as the sole bearer-like selector and warns that logging it can become cross-user access. The route is anonymous, a fresh profile defaults consent off, and the exception-to-log flow is direct; only the browser response is sanitized.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: The browser receives a generic INVALID_REQUEST response and no stack trace, but log redaction is absent; UUID entropy does not help after the value is copied into logs.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Candidate-local narrative includes counterevidence where available; centralized attack-path analysis remains required.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Anyone with application or aggregated-log read access can recover a device capability and use the public profile API to read or alter that device's stored country, allergies, favorites, aliases, and memos.

candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Focused centralized validation remains recommended.

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-01:W01-A006-C01] The deviceId path segment on the anonymous POST /api/v1/profiles/{deviceId}/allergies endpoint; ordinary clients also send their real bearer-like identifier here.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C019] The profile deviceId path value is a bearer-like capability chosen/generated by the client and supplied unchanged to POST /profiles/{deviceId}/allergies.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-008] Possession of deviceId grants profile read/write authority; a legitimate or malicious request supplies it.

[worker-04:W04-P031-C01] The public profile path contains a browser-generated high-entropy deviceId whose possession authorizes profile reads and writes.

evidence.control: The handler sanitizes the HTTP response but has no sensitive-field classification or redaction before logging the internal exception message.
evidence.impact_path: Path bearer ID -> ProfileService exception message -> RuntimeException.getMessage -> application log -> log reader can reuse the ID against anonymous profile CRUD.
evidence.preconditions: The profile has rememberAllergies=false (the default), the request body passes validation, and an unintended party later gains access to application or aggregated logs.
evidence.severity_facts: The value is a bearer capability rather than a password and profile data is intentionally limited, but possession permits anonymous read/write of allergy and saved-place state. The issue is server-runtime logging, not merely a nested developer-worktree artifact.
evidence.sink: SLF4J warning log at GlobalExceptionHandler.handled line 38.
evidence.source: A legitimate or attacker-influenced anonymous allergy-create request carrying a profile deviceId.

evidence.boundary: See affected locations and threat model.
evidence.control: The browser receives a generic INVALID_REQUEST response and no stack trace, but log redaction is absent; UUID entropy does not help after the value is copied into logs.
evidence.counterevidence: Candidate-local narrative includes counterevidence where available; centralized attack-path analysis remains required.
evidence.impact_path: Anyone with application or aggregated-log read access can recover a device capability and use the public profile API to read or alter that device's stored country, allergies, favorites, aliases, and memos.
evidence.preconditions: A user or scripted client posts an allergy before consent. The server rejects it but logs 'profile <deviceId> has not opted in'. A log reader replays that capability against profile endpoints. Counterevidence: log access is required and the design already grants full authority to anyone who possesses the high-entropy ID.
evidence.sink: When consent is off, ProfileService concatenates the full deviceId into ApiException.getMessage(); GlobalExceptionHandler logs that internal message verbatim.
evidence.source: The profile deviceId path value is a bearer-like capability chosen/generated by the client and supplied unchanged to POST /profiles/{deviceId}/allergies.

evidence.boundary: backend/src/main/java/com/mermaid/profile/ProfileService.java

evidence.counterevidence: deviceId is high entropy and is not enumerable from public responses.
evidence.impact_path: Possession of deviceId grants profile read/write authority; a legitimate or malicious request supplies it. -> The capability value enters application logs on add-allergy while consent is off. -> Anyone with log access can reuse the identifier to read or alter allergy/favorite state.
preconditions: A user attempts allergy creation while consent is false and another actor can read logs.

evidence.evidence: ProfileController lines 48-53 bind deviceId from the anonymous path. ProfileService lines 66-72 call getOrCreate, observe default-off consent, and build the message 'profile ' + deviceId + ' has not opted in'. ApiException line 27 supplies that string to RuntimeException. GlobalExceptionHandler line 38 logs e.getMessage() with SLF4J. UserProfile lines 41-48 confirm the default-off state.
evidence.method: Static source-to-sink trace over immutable be68c887 blobs that are byte-identical to the outer 654f906e target counterparts; no later centralized validation phase or live request was run.

evidence.evidence: Issue a synthetic POST for a test device with rememberAllergies=false and capture logs, then confirm the full deviceId appears and assess log-reader roles/retention. No real profile identifier is needed.
evidence.method: Static candidate-local review.
evidence.proof_gap: Focused centralized validation remains recommended.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A006-C01] ApiException documents its message as internal and GlobalExceptionHandler replaces it with a safe browser sentence, but there is no equivalent log redaction; line 38 logs the sensitive internal message itself.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C019] The browser receives a generic INVALID_REQUEST response and no stack trace, but log redaction is absent; UUID entropy does not help after the value is copied into logs.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-008] ProfileService embeds deviceId in ApiException and GlobalExceptionHandler logs the raw message.

[worker-04:W04-P031-C01] The HTTP response substitutes safe fixed copy, but no equivalent log redaction or capability hashing exists.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A006-C01] Application logs retain a capability that is sufficient to read and mutate the corresponding anonymous profile, exposing or altering saved allergies, consent state, country, favorites, aliases, and memos to anyone who later obtains those logs.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C019] Anyone with application or aggregated-log read access can recover a device capability and use the public profile API to read or alter that device's stored country, allergies, favorites, aliases, and memos.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-008] Anyone with log access can reuse the identifier to read or alter allergy/favorite state.

[worker-04:W04-P031-C01] Anyone with lower-privileged access to retained or forwarded logs can recover a profile bearer capability and read or mutate allergy, consent, favorite, alias, and memo data through anonymous CRUD endpoints.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: Consent/profile exception paths include a reusable bearer-like device identifier in application logs. Merge boundary: Medium: W04's canonical source absorbs several equivalent current/nested exception paths; unknown-subpath logging remains separate.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: ProfileController line 18 defines deviceId in the route prefix; GlobalExceptionHandler line 58 logs e.getResourcePath(); the resolved Spring 6.2.19 resource handler passes getPath(request) directly into NoResourceFoundException.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Use MockMvc against /api/v1/profiles/<uuid>/unknown and capture the configured logger to confirm the exact leading path form; the sensitive UUID remains in either full-path form.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The client receives a generic not-found message, but the log call has no route templating, segment redaction, or capability filter.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A typo, stale client route, or probing request copies a profile authorization capability into application logs, allowing a log reader to access allergy and favorite data through the public profile API.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The handler's own documentation says it receives every path no controller claims. Spring 6.2.19 ResourceHttpRequestHandler constructs NoResourceFoundException from PATH_WITHIN_HANDLER_MAPPING_ATTRIBUTE, which retains the requested profile path.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: The HTTP error envelope is sanitized, but there is no sensitive-field classification or log redaction for PublicApiException messages.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: lat/lng query -> facility client failure wrapper -> RuntimeException message -> application/aggregated log.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The deployment uses DATA_MODE=live, a public API failure occurs, and an unintended party later reads application or aggregated logs.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The disclosure is limited to a failure path and a supported non-default data mode, but exact care-search location is sensitive; the threat model classifies address/memo leakage in logs as medium-impact evidence.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: FacilityController lines 35-43 bind lat/lng. HospitalApiClient lines 85-93 and PharmacyApiClient lines 88-105 append those doubles to their live-mode PublicApiException messages. PublicApiException passes the message to RuntimeException, and GlobalExceptionHandler line 64 logs the throwable. DataModeProperties lines 25-31 confirms LIVE is supported even though HYBRID is the development default.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static request-to-log trace over immutable be68c887 blobs byte-identical to outer target 654f906e; no live facility request was issued.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The browser receives only a generic SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE sentence, but that control applies after the sensitive coordinates have already been placed in the server log.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Application and aggregated logs retain precise locations associated with medical-facility searches, exposing sensitive user movement or care-seeking context to log readers and increasing breach scope.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Both pharmacy and hospital routes are anonymous and the LIVE data mode is an explicitly supported deployment mode. Any upstream status error, decode error, or transport failure after coordinates are accepted reaches the wrapper and centralized log synchronously.

candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: JSON string -> Jackson-decoded LF -> FieldError.rejectedValue -> exception message -> multi-line log output.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The endpoint is reachable and the application uses ordinary line-oriented Logback output, as configured by the pinned Spring Boot logging stack.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The value length is bounded and the effect is log integrity rather than code execution, but exploitation is unauthenticated, deterministic, and does not require an upstream failure.

Spring MethodArgumentNotValidException lines 93-105 concatenates FieldError.toString; FieldError lines 127-143 preserves rejectedValue; Logback MessageConverter lines 25-26 returns getFormattedMessage unchanged; GlobalExceptionHandler line 87 is the sink.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static framework-assisted trace using pinned Spring 6.2.19 and Logback 1.5.34 sources; no request was issued.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The Size constraints cap the payload and the browser response contains only a field name, but neither control neutralizes line separators in the server log.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A remote unauthenticated requester can forge warning-log lines or corrupt line-oriented ingestion, weakening monitoring and incident-response evidence without successfully mutating any profile.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Jackson decodes JSON \n to a real LF before Bean Validation. The pinned Spring FieldError implementation includes that value verbatim, and the request is rejected before any authorization-like device ownership or database mutation matters.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: An unauthenticated caller supplies an invalid string, including escaped newline characters, for a typed JSON field such as favorite facilityType; malformed chat JSON is a sibling source.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Unauthenticated JSON-to-Java deserialization boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Typed DTO conversion rejects the value but preserves it in diagnostic exceptions; the centralized handler lacks a safe dedicated conversion-error branch.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Jackson truncates long diagnostic strings, limiting volume, and the browser receives only a generic error. The candidate concerns server-side log confidentiality and integrity.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Invalid typed JSON -> Jackson value-bearing exception -> Spring HttpMessageNotReadableException -> catch-all log.error -> sensitive/log-forged record.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The deployed logging encoder preserves message control characters and logs are retained or consumed as line-oriented records.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Full exception log at GlobalExceptionHandler line 101.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Unauthenticated JSON-to-Java deserialization boundary.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: GlobalExceptionHandler lines 79-85 omit HttpMessageNotReadableException and line 101 logs the Throwable. Jackson 2.21.4 DeserializationContext.weirdStringException includes _quotedString(value), whose DatabindContext implementation truncates but does not escape control characters.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Capture a MockMvc request with facilityType set to an invalid string containing an escaped newline to verify the exact Spring wrapper/appender output and whether an external log pipeline normalizes it.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The catch-all returns a generic INTERNAL_ERROR response, but that client-side redaction does not sanitize the Throwable passed to log.error.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Attacker text and potentially sensitive malformed request content enters application logs; CR/LF-bearing values can corrupt log record boundaries and create misleading audit entries.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Jackson 2.21.4 DeserializationContext constructs invalid-string errors with the quoted source value, retaining up to the first and last 500 characters and explicitly not escaping linefeeds. Spring wraps that cause in HttpMessageNotReadableException, which this handler does not list.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: The itemSeq path segment, including percent-encoded control characters accepted and decoded by the pinned embedded Tomcat stack.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Raw HTTP target validation sees only legal percent-encoding; neither the String path binding nor exception/log layer strips decoded control characters.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: encoded itemSeq -> Tomcat path decode -> DrugService NotFoundException message -> Logback output with physical newline.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The request reaches embedded Tomcat without a stricter reverse proxy and the live detail lookup returns an ordinary empty result rather than a provider error.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: No data or privilege is gained, and a stricter edge proxy may reject the request, but the endpoint is unauthenticated and the direct Tomcat deployment accepts the encoding; impact is audit/log integrity.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: GlobalExceptionHandler.notFound warning log at line 44.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Anonymous drug-detail path containing an encoded newline and forged log content.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: DrugController accepts String itemSeq and DrugService appends it at lines 143-148. Tomcat Parameters/UDecoder and CoyoteAdapter sources show percent decoding before path mapping and no LF rejection during normalization. NotFoundException calls super(message), GlobalExceptionHandler line 44 logs it, and Logback MessageConverter returns the formatted message unchanged.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static trace using immutable repository sources plus pinned Tomcat 10.1.55 and Logback 1.5.34 source; no network request was sent.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: SLF4J parameter placeholders prevent format-string interpretation, but Logback's MessageConverter emits event.getFormattedMessage() unchanged and therefore does not neutralize embedded line breaks.
absorbed_provenance.impact: An unauthenticated requester can inject additional physical log lines and attacker-chosen prefixes into warning logs, misleading operators, corrupting line-oriented parsers, or hiding the true event during incident response.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Tomcat 10.1.55 validates the raw percent-encoded target, then UDecoder converts %xx and CoyoteAdapter normalization rejects null/backslash/traversal but not LF. A successful no-result upstream response reaches the NotFoundException branch in supported live mode.

evidence.control: DrugService embeds itemSeq in NotFoundException and context-failure messages; handlers log messages verbatim.
evidence.counterevidence: Raw HTTP control characters are normally rejected by the server.
evidence.impact_path: Anonymous callers control the unvalidated itemSeq path string. -> A decoded control-character value can create attacker-chosen log lines or pollute structured records. -> Incident correlation can be corrupted and malicious text can enter log collectors.
preconditions: Container accepts a crafted encoded path value that decodes to log control characters.
evidence.sink: A decoded control-character value can create attacker-chosen log lines or pollute structured records.
evidence.source: Anonymous callers control the unvalidated itemSeq path string.

evidence.evidence: DrugController accepts String itemSeq and DrugService appends it at lines 143-148. Tomcat Parameters/UDecoder and CoyoteAdapter sources show percent decoding before path mapping and no LF rejection during normalization. NotFoundException calls super(message), GlobalExceptionHandler line 44 logs it, and Logback MessageConverter returns the formatted message unchanged.
evidence.method: Static trace using immutable repository sources plus pinned Tomcat 10.1.55 and Logback 1.5.34 source; no network request was sent.

evidence.evidence: No validation or explicit log encoding exists between path value and SLF4J arguments.
evidence.method: Static source-to-log trace.
evidence.proof_gap: Tomcat/Spring acceptance of percent-encoded CR/LF in this exact route must be confirmed; oversized benign pollution is still possible within request-target limits.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A007-C03] SLF4J parameter placeholders prevent format-string interpretation, but Logback's MessageConverter emits event.getFormattedMessage() unchanged and therefore does not neutralize embedded line breaks.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-027] DrugService embeds itemSeq in NotFoundException and context-failure messages; handlers log messages verbatim.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A007-C03] An unauthenticated requester can inject additional physical log lines and attacker-chosen prefixes into warning logs, misleading operators, corrupting line-oriented parsers, or hiding the true event during incident response.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-027] Incident correlation can be corrupted and malicious text can enter log collectors.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: The drug path identifier lacks an adequate control-character/length boundary before being embedded in logged exception text. Merge boundary: Low: both candidates share the same endpoint, control, and log-integrity sink.

title: Unbounded drug identifiers reach log-forging sinks
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A007-C03] The detail service embeds itemSeq in a NotFoundException and the centralized not-found handler logs the message without CR/LF neutralization.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-027] A decoded control-character value can create attacker-chosen log lines or pollute structured records.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: The resultCode and resultMsg fields in a malicious or compromised public-API error envelope, an explicitly untrusted upstream boundary in the threat model.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: TLS and fixed base URLs authenticate the destination but do not make response error text safe for logs; requireOk checks only the code value.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: upstream JSON resultMsg -> PublicApiException message -> SLF4J/Logback output without line encoding.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: A public API or its delivery path is malicious/compromised or emits attacker-influenced diagnostics, and an anonymous request exercises that adapter.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: This needs an upstream-boundary compromise and is therefore weaker than direct user log injection; the response limit bounds per-event amplification. It is not evidence of DATA_GO_KR_SERVICE_KEY disclosure.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Hybrid adapter warning logs or GlobalExceptionHandler's live-mode throwable log.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Malicious or compromised provider response with a non-00 code and crafted resultMsg.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: PublicApiResponse lines 55-70 reads resultMsg and appends it unchanged. PharmacyApiClient line 101 logs e.getMessage in hybrid mode; in live mode line 104 wraps it and GlobalExceptionHandler line 64 prints the cause chain. The 4 MiB WebClient cap is the only size bound located.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static upstream-to-log trace over immutable target-equivalent blobs; no claim was made that the current government service emits malicious text or echoes a service key.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: WebClient caps buffered responses at 4 MiB and the browser sees a generic error or fixture provenance, but log output applies no character neutralization to provider diagnostics.
absorbed_provenance.impact: A compromised upstream can forge log records, inject misleading incident text, or amplify each failing request into a large log entry, undermining audit integrity and operational response.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Every public-API adapter calls requireOk on HTTP-200 error envelopes. Hybrid is the default and logs e.getMessage directly; live preserves the exception as a cause and logs the full stack. The response byte limit bounds but does not sanitize resultMsg.

affected_locations.detail: Anonymous health-related chat content enters the retrieval path.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Retained product names are bounded and must be literal substrings of the user's text; retained ingredients are model-derived from that health request.
affected_locations.label: source_validation

affected_locations.detail: The retriever logs the accepted ingredient and product-name lists at INFO, including suppressed model proposals when an allergy is declared.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The com.mermaid package is configured at DEBUG, so INFO retrieval-term records are enabled by the application configuration.
affected_locations.label: configuration_boundary

affected_locations.detail: Extractor rejection/failure logs deliberately emit only codes and counts, but the downstream retriever logs values for accepted results.
affected_locations.label: closest_control

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A chat user supplies medicine/product text and symptom context; accepted Korean product terms are exact substrings of that input, while the model infers ingredient terms from the same health-related request.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Length/count/control-character validation and value-free extractor error logging, with no downstream redaction for accepted values.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: health-related chat -> accepted product/inferred ingredient terms -> INFO interpolation -> retained/aggregated logs -> operator or log-reader learns medication/allergy context
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: INFO logs are retained or exported and an operator/log reader can correlate the record to a request or user through timing or surrounding infrastructure metadata.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The direct line contains terms rather than a full transcript or explicit identity, so sensitivity and identifiability depend on log retention and correlation context. No log-forging claim is made because accepted values are bounded and control characters are rejected.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: INFO application log records in DrugContextRetriever, potentially forwarded to a centralized log store.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: User-provided medication names and model-derived ingredient selections tied to an anonymous health consultation, including an allergy-declaration path.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: SearchTermExtractor lines 211-217 binds retained product names to user text and lines 220-243 keeps rejected logs value-free. DrugContextRetriever lines 160-163 and 178-180 emit accepted/suppressed lists at INFO. application.yml lines 60-62 enables INFO through a DEBUG package level.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable static source-to-log trace plus full review of SearchTermExtractorTest at target 654f906e; no health text was submitted and no application log was generated.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Terms are capped, control characters are rejected, and extractor error logs omit raw values, which limits log injection and volume. Those checks do not prevent accepted medicine names from being retained in logs; no redaction or hashing is applied at the sink.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Medication names and allergy-related inferred choices can persist in local or aggregated application logs and become visible to operators, support tooling, or a later log compromise. Even without an account identifier in this log line, timing and infrastructure metadata can make the health context correlatable.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: SearchTermExtractorTest explicitly verifies that rejected terms are logged only as codes/counts and not values, showing the intended privacy-aware boundary. The successful path then passes the values to DrugContextRetriever, whose INFO statements print both lists. The configured package level enables those statements.

DrugContextRetriever lines 160-163 and 178-180 emit accepted/suppressed lists at INFO. application.yml lines 60-62 enables INFO through a DEBUG package level.
evidence.method: Immutable static source-to-log trace plus full review of SearchTermExtractorTest at target 654f906e; no health text was submitted and no application log was generated.

evidence.evidence: INFO templates include `extracted.ingredientsEn()`, `query.ingredientsEn()`, and `query.productNamesKo()` as value-bearing arguments.
evidence.method: Static inspection of every retrieval log statement and its data origin.
evidence.proof_gap: The deployed logger destination, access policy, redaction layer, and retention period were not available, so actual disclosure scope is environment-dependent.

evidence.evidence: The log statements interpolate search terms derived from the active conversation.
evidence.method: Immutable full-file log inventory.
evidence.proof_gap: Model-derived ingredients are indirect rather than the verbatim symptom sentence.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C03] Terms are capped, control characters are rejected, and extractor error logs omit raw values, which limits log injection and volume. Those checks do not prevent accepted medicine names from being retained in logs; no redaction or hashing is applied at the sink.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C007] The raw symptom sentence is not logged here; several nearby events log only counts, timings, and fixed violation codes.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-010] Retrieval logging has no redaction or privacy classification.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C03] Medication names and allergy-related inferred choices can persist in local or aggregated application logs and become visible to operators, support tooling, or a later log compromise. Even without an account identifier in this log line, timing and infrastructure metadata can make the health context correlatable.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C007] Log readers, log exports, or over-retained telemetry can expose inferred conditions, medicine use, or allergy context that the service otherwise promises not to persist as consultation transcripts.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-010] Health-related query data persists beyond the consultation session in server logs.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: Medication and allergy-derived retrieval terms are written as values rather than privacy-preserving codes or counts. Merge boundary: Low: same routine logging sink and health-context disclosure.

title: Accepted health-related search terms are logged
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A029-C03] DrugContextRetriever interpolates accepted and allergy-suppressed search-term values into INFO application logs instead of retaining SearchTermExtractor's value-free code/count logging discipline.

[worker-02:R01W02-S03-C007] Potentially health-revealing terms are interpolated into persistent INFO logs rather than reduced to counts or non-sensitive event identifiers.

[worker-03:W03-CAND-010] DrugContextRetriever writes dropped ingredient/product terms at INFO/WARN.

affected_locations.detail: The public WebClient sets DNS resolution and a 4 MiB decode cap but no response, read, or whole-request timeout.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: A representative anonymous route synchronously reaches the public client after only a 200-character query bound.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The request ends in block(); fallback/error handling runs only after the reactive operation terminates with an error.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Hospital paging repeatedly invokes bodyToMono(...).block() without a deadline.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Fan-out parks blocking calls on the shared boundedElastic scheduler and waits for all results, so stalled responses retain shared workers.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

affected_locations.detail: LLM operations apply explicit whole-Mono timeouts, demonstrating that the shared HttpClient alone does not supply the missing public-call deadline.
affected_locations.label: counterexample

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A malicious or degraded Naver/data.go.kr upstream can accept a request and delay headers/body completion or trickle fewer than 4 MiB indefinitely; anonymous callers can concurrently trigger the affected public routes.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Fixed authorities, disabled redirects, 4 MiB body cap, finite connect/acquire phases, but no established-response deadline.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Anonymous trigger -> connection established -> upstream withholds completion -> no response timeout -> workers/connections remain occupied -> concurrent legitimate requests queue or fail -> health-information outage.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Hybrid/live operation with configured provider credentials, an upstream or network path that can keep responses incomplete, and no shorter external request deadline that reliably cancels the outbound subscription.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The malicious-upstream actor is explicit in the threat model. Exact service-wide saturation depends on JVM CPU-derived scheduler/pool sizes and deployment ingress cancellation, so controlled measurement is still recommended.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Synchronous block() waits on servlet or boundedElastic workers for the upstream Mono to complete.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Malicious/degraded upstream response timing plus concurrent anonymous requests to public-data routes.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Static target source proves the public client lacks a deadline and all representative consumers block. Local resolved dependency source distinguishes the finite 30s connect and 45s acquire phases from the unset response timeout. No backend rate/admission component was found; deployment-layer timeouts remain the principal counterevidence to check.
candidate_local_validation.method: Point the operator-owned public/geocode base URL at a controlled stub that accepts requests and never completes the response, trigger several anonymous routes, and measure servlet threads, boundedElastic workers, active/pending connections, and recovery. Confirm production ingress/request deadlines separately.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: MAX_IN_MEMORY_BYTES bounds decoded body size, Reactor/Netty bound connection establishment and pending pool acquisition by default, per-operation row/fan-out limits bound completed work, and LLM callers add Mono.timeout. None bounds an established public-API response.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Stalled responses can retain servlet request threads, boundedElastic workers, pooled connections, and waiting requests long enough to deny geocode, drug, and facility information to other users and potentially degrade the whole backend.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Resolved Reactor Netty 1.2.18 source leaves responseTimeout null and redirect following false unless explicitly enabled; resolved Netty supplies a 30-second connect timeout, while the default pool acquisition budget is 45 seconds. The repository config never sets a response deadline for publicApiWebClient, and hybrid fallback cannot execute until the wait fails. The 4 MiB cap stops a fast oversized body but not a slow incomplete one.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: A malicious or degraded Naver/data.go.kr upstream can accept a request and delay headers/body completion or trickle fewer than 4 MiB indefinitely; anonymous callers can concurrently trigger the affected public routes.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Fixed authorities, disabled redirects, 4 MiB body cap, finite connect/acquire phases, but no established-response deadline.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Anonymous trigger -> connection established -> upstream withholds completion -> no response timeout -> workers/connections remain occupied -> concurrent legitimate requests queue or fail -> health-information outage.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Hybrid/live operation with configured provider credentials, an upstream or network path that can keep responses incomplete, and no shorter external request deadline that reliably cancels the outbound subscription.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The malicious-upstream actor is explicit in the threat model. Exact service-wide saturation depends on JVM CPU-derived scheduler/pool sizes and deployment ingress cancellation, so controlled measurement is still recommended.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Synchronous block() waits on servlet or boundedElastic workers for the upstream Mono to complete.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Malicious/degraded upstream response timing plus concurrent anonymous requests to public-data routes.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Static target source proves the public client lacks a deadline and all representative consumers block. Local resolved dependency source distinguishes the finite 30s connect and 45s acquire phases from the unset response timeout. No backend rate/admission component was found; deployment-layer timeouts remain the principal counterevidence to check.
candidate_local_validation.method: Point the operator-owned public/geocode base URL at a controlled stub that accepts requests and never completes the response, trigger several anonymous routes, and measure servlet threads, boundedElastic workers, active/pending connections, and recovery. Confirm production ingress/request deadlines separately.

source_record.closest_control: MAX_IN_MEMORY_BYTES bounds decoded body size, Reactor/Netty bound connection establishment and pending pool acquisition by default, per-operation row/fan-out limits bound completed work, and LLM callers add Mono.timeout. None bounds an established public-API response.
source_record.impact: Stalled responses can retain servlet request threads, boundedElastic workers, pooled connections, and waiting requests long enough to deny geocode, drug, and facility information to other users and potentially degrade the whole backend.
source_record.plausibility: Resolved Reactor Netty 1.2.18 source leaves responseTimeout null and redirect following false unless explicitly enabled; resolved Netty supplies a 30-second connect timeout, while the default pool acquisition budget is 45 seconds. The repository config never sets a response deadline for publicApiWebClient, and hybrid fallback cannot execute until the wait fails. The 4 MiB cap stops a fast oversized body but not a slow incomplete one.
source_record.title: Public API responses can hold blocking server work without an application deadline
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The shared public WebClient has no response/read/overall timeout and every reviewed public client waits with block(). A connect timeout only bounds establishment, and a pool-acquire timeout only bounds queued acquisition; neither releases an established slow response.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: No `responseTimeout`, Netty read/write timeout handler, Reactor `.timeout`, or timed `.block(Duration)` exists on either hospital path.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Paging caps, concurrency caps, and exception fallback run only after a call terminates or throws; they do not end a connection that never completes.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Stalled upstream responses can hold request threads and detail worker tasks indefinitely; repeated public requests can exhaust the service even though page and candidate counts are bounded.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: WebClientConfig builds a bare HttpClient resolver. HospitalApiClient fetchPage and HospitalDetailApiClient findByYkiho terminate in unbounded block calls. No enclosing timeout is present in FacilityService.

source_record.closest_control: Paging caps, concurrency caps, and exception fallback run only after a call terminates or throws; they do not end a connection that never completes.
source_record.impact: Stalled upstream responses can hold request threads and detail worker tasks indefinitely; repeated public requests can exhaust the service even though page and candidate counts are bounded.
source_record.plausibility: WebClientConfig builds a bare HttpClient resolver. HospitalApiClient fetchPage and HospitalDetailApiClient findByYkiho terminate in unbounded block calls. No enclosing timeout is present in FacilityService.
source_record.title: Hospital list and detail clients have no response timeout
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The shared publicApiWebClient config has no connect/response/read timeout, and both hospital adapters call `.block()` without a reactive timeout.

affected_locations.detail: The public Spring MVC request synchronously waits for GeocodeClient.search to return.
affected_locations.label: public_blocking_entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: The request terminates in Mono.block(); exception handling runs only after an error arrives and supplies no duration bound.
affected_locations.label: unbounded_blocking_sink

affected_locations.detail: The shared Reactor Netty HttpClient configures DNS resolution and the WebClient configures a 4 MiB body bound, but neither sets connect, response, read, or overall request timeouts.
affected_locations.label: missing_transport_deadline

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: An anonymous caller can create many geocode requests that enter the blocking upstream path. The caller does not need to control Naver; exploitation becomes strongest during a provider or network condition that accepts connections but delays or never completes responses.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Anonymous geocode requests plus untrusted upstream response timing at the external-service boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Bounded query/body/result sizes and exception handling, but no temporal or aggregate-work bound.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Normal fast Naver responses complete promptly, transport/platform defaults may eventually fail some connection states, and an external ingress could impose a deadline. None of those controls is visible or guaranteed by the reviewed application, so a hanging-response test is needed before final severity.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Provider/network stalls after connection acceptance -> attacker supplies concurrent geocode requests -> each servlet request blocks without an application deadline -> pending threads/connections accumulate -> the application cannot serve legitimate requests.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Naver credentials are configured, the upstream or network exhibits sufficiently slow/half-open behavior, callers can reach the route concurrently, and no deployment-level timeout or bulkhead outside the repository closes requests sooner.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Spring MVC request threads and Reactor Netty outbound connections retained by Mono.block() while awaiting Naver.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Anonymous geocode requests plus untrusted upstream response timing at the external-service boundary.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: At revision 654f906e00e81648d1482210b6a9171747dddd75, GeocodeClient lines 45-52 ends the upstream operation with block(), and WebClientConfig lines 42-60 constructs the relevant HttpClient/WebClient without response/read/overall timeout configuration. The class comment at WebClientConfig lines 10-12 confirms Spring MVC servlet operation.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Use a controlled upstream that accepts the connection and never sends headers/body, then call /api/v1/geocode concurrently and observe whether requests remain pending, servlet threads accumulate, and any platform timeout outside the repository terminates them. External proxy/container deadlines could reduce exploitability.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Input length validation, the 4 MiB response-memory cap, generic exception translation, and a five-result parse cap limit other dimensions. None schedules cancellation or releases the blocking servlet request after a bounded time.
absorbed_provenance.impact: During a slow or half-open upstream incident, concurrent anonymous requests can retain servlet threads and outbound connections long enough to exhaust application capacity, denying unrelated API functionality including emergency and medicine information.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: High for the missing-deadline code path and medium for deliberate remote exploitation because the long stall depends on upstream/network behavior not shown under caller control. It remains operationally security-relevant: public callers can multiply any single upstream stall into application-wide resource pressure.
