affected_locations.detail: Redis is started without an ACL/password configuration and published on host port 6379 without a loopback-only host address.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint

affected_locations.detail: Six-hour Redis values use polymorphic JSON restricted to application and Java utility types; this blocks arbitrary classes but provides no cache writer authentication or integrity check.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Government-published drug contraindication warnings are read from a predictable durWarnings cache.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Pharmacy rows and LIVE/FIXTURE provenance are read from a predictable coordinate-keyed cache and can influence rendered facility facts.
affected_locations.label: sink

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A LAN peer or co-resident process able to reach the developer host's published TCP/6379 port can issue Redis protocol commands without credentials.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: No Redis authentication or loopback-only port binding; the type allowlist constrains object classes but not command authorization or the correctness of allowed application records.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: reachable 6379 -> nopass +@all Redis command -> DEL/FLUSH causes repeated cache misses and quota pressure, or SET of a valid allowlisted cache value -> altered warning/facility/provenance output.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The local Compose Redis port is reachable through the host firewall/network. Integrity exploitation additionally requires reproducing a known Spring cache key and the documented polymorphic JSON shape; availability exploitation needs only administrative Redis commands.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: This Redis instance is distinct from MariaDB and holds public-API caches rather than allergy/profile rows. The current development runtime proves unauthenticated command capability, but no production deployment is established. No RCE, arbitrary-class gadget, or secret extraction claim is made.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Six-hour public-API caches consumed by drug and facility services, plus the shared upstream quota and latency those caches protect.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: A network peer with route/firewall access to a developer machine running the Redis service.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: compose.yaml lines 35-44 supplies no auth and publishes 6379. Current Docker metadata shows HostIp empty and IPv4/IPv6 all-interface publication. Redis returned protected-mode=no, bind=* -::* and ACL 'user default on nopass ... +@all'; the host-port PING returned +PONG. CacheConfig lines 35-52 allows only app/utility types, and @Cacheable sites include durWarnings and pharmaciesNear.v2.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable source trace plus read-only Docker inspect, Redis CONFIG/ACL queries, and a PING. No key enumeration, cache read, SET, DEL, FLUSH, or other datastore mutation was performed.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: CacheConfig's BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator restricts deserialization to com.mermaid and selected Java packages, which materially narrows gadget risk; TTL limits a normal forged entry to six hours. Neither control authenticates Redis writers or prevents eviction. No concrete gadget was found, so remote code execution is explicitly not claimed.
absorbed_provenance.impact: An attacker can evict caches repeatedly to force expensive government-API refetches and consume shared quota, or SET correctly typed values under known cache keys to replace drug warnings, pharmacy hours/results, or provenance until expiry. This can degrade availability and make safety-relevant cached facts incorrect.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The current runtime publishes 6379 on 0.0.0.0 and [::]. Read-only CONFIG/ACL queries returned protected-mode=no, bind=* -::*, and default on nopass with +@all; a benign RESP PING through 127.0.0.1:6379 returned PONG. Cache names, key expressions, and allowed record classes are visible in source, while ACL +@all includes DEL, FLUSH*, and SET.

attacker_controlled_source:
[worker-01:W01-A017-C03] A LAN peer or co-resident process able to reach the developer host's published TCP/6379 port can issue Redis protocol commands without credentials.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C018] Any host reachable over the developer machine's published TCP interfaces can connect to Redis without credentials.

evidence.control: No Redis authentication or loopback-only port binding; the type allowlist constrains object classes but not command authorization or the correctness of allowed application records.
evidence.impact_path: reachable 6379 -> nopass +@all Redis command -> DEL/FLUSH causes repeated cache misses and quota pressure, or SET of a valid allowlisted cache value -> altered warning/facility/provenance output.
evidence.preconditions: The local Compose Redis port is reachable through the host firewall/network. Integrity exploitation additionally requires reproducing a known Spring cache key and the documented polymorphic JSON shape; availability exploitation needs only administrative Redis commands.
evidence.severity_facts: This Redis instance is distinct from MariaDB and holds public-API caches rather than allergy/profile rows. The current development runtime proves unauthenticated command capability, but no production deployment is established. No RCE, arbitrary-class gadget, or secret extraction claim is made.
evidence.sink: Six-hour public-API caches consumed by drug and facility services, plus the shared upstream quota and latency those caches protect.
evidence.source: A network peer with route/firewall access to a developer machine running the Redis service.

evidence.evidence: compose.yaml lines 35-44 supplies no auth and publishes 6379. Current Docker metadata shows HostIp empty and IPv4/IPv6 all-interface publication. Redis returned protected-mode=no, bind=* -::* and ACL 'user default on nopass ... +@all'; the host-port PING returned +PONG. CacheConfig lines 35-52 allows only app/utility types, and @Cacheable sites include durWarnings and pharmaciesNear.v2.
evidence.method: Immutable source trace plus read-only Docker inspect, Redis CONFIG/ACL queries, and a PING. No key enumeration, cache read, SET, DEL, FLUSH, or other datastore mutation was performed.

closest_control:
[worker-01:W01-A017-C03] CacheConfig's BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator restricts deserialization to com.mermaid and selected Java packages, which materially narrows gadget risk; TTL limits a normal forged entry to six hours. Neither control authenticates Redis writers or prevents eviction. No concrete gadget was found, so remote code execution is explicitly not claimed.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C018] Local-development intent, host firewall/network isolation, six-hour TTL, and the polymorphic-type allowlist reduce deployment reachability and gadget scope but do not authenticate Redis.

[worker-05:R01W05-C031] The file labels itself local development infrastructure, so exploitability depends on host-network exposure. Docker/OS firewalling and a trusted laptop network are deployment controls, not repository-enforced binds.
impact:
[worker-01:W01-A017-C03] An attacker can evict caches repeatedly to force expensive government-API refetches and consume shared quota, or SET correctly typed values under known cache keys to replace drug warnings, pharmacy hours/results, or provenance until expiry. This can degrade availability and make safety-relevant cached facts incorrect.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C018] A network-adjacent attacker can read, delete, or replace cache entries, drive repeated upstream quota misses, and potentially poison server-trusted drug/facility cache data.

[worker-05:R01W05-C031] A hostile LAN/local peer can flush caches, cause deserialization failures, or write attacker-chosen cache values, degrading or corrupting verified facility/drug responses and multiplying upstream quota use.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: All-interface nopass Redis with broad commands exposes cache availability and integrity to reachable peers. Merge boundary: Low: same datastore root; nested cache-poisoning exploitability remains separate.

title: Development Redis is published without authentication
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A017-C03] The running image disables protected mode, binds all interfaces, and leaves the default user nopass with +@all. Compose supplies no password, ACL, TLS, or loopback host binding, so Docker publication exposes administrative cache commands.

[worker-02:R01W02-S04-C018] Compose publishes 6379 without a loopback bind, password, ACL, or protected-mode setting; the official container runtime here reports protected-mode=no and default user nopass ~* &* +@all.

[worker-05:R01W05-C031] `6379:6379` publishes Redis on host interfaces and the image is started with defaults. Spring trusts it as the shared cache for government responses.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: Any process or adjacent-network actor able to reach the Compose-published Redis port can issue Redis write commands without repository-configured credentials. Internet reachability is deployment-dependent and not proven by this checkout.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Unauthenticated Redis command channel exposed by the default Compose port mapping, conditional on network reachability.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Polymorphic package allowlist; it controls class selection but not who wrote an allowed object or whether its medical fields came from a provider.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: The repository labels Compose local-development infrastructure, and the threat model says production network controls are outside the repository. There is no public HTTP cache-write endpoint, and the package validator prevents arbitrary non-allowlisted class names; these facts lower reachability/confidence but do not close a reachable Redis deployment.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Actor reaches Redis -> writes an expected cache key with an allowed application-record payload -> Spring cache deserializes it -> API client body is skipped -> DrugService trusts the forged record -> server emits false medicine facts with MFDS provenance.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The deployed Redis endpoint is reachable by the actor, has no external ACL/TLS/auth override, and the attacker crafts the serializer's expected JSON/type metadata and cache key.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer followed by @Cacheable medicine clients, DrugService allergy/provenance assembly, and public drug/chat responses.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Unauthenticated Redis command channel exposed by the default Compose port mapping, conditional on network reachability.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: At be68c887fe118d02d2bfac1987a0c51aa62abbb6, compose.yaml lines 35-42 publishes unauthenticated Redis; application.yml lines 35-46 configures no credential; CacheConfig lines 35-52 accepts com.mermaid polymorphic records; DrugPermissionApiClient lines 62-85 consumes Redis-cached government records; DrugService lines 338-361 turns them into medicine facts and server provenance.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Validate the intended production network/Redis ACL configuration and, in an isolated local stack, write one serializer-compatible Permitted cache value to confirm a public drug request consumes it and labels it as government data.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator restricts class names to application/JDK packages, which narrows gadget exposure but is not an authenticity boundary. Docker/network isolation may contain Redis in production, but no such production evidence is present and Compose publishes the port on the host.
absorbed_provenance.impact: An actor with Redis network reachability can persist false product names, ingredients, licence state, prescription status, narratives, or warnings for attacker-chosen cache keys. The application can then use those values in allergy decisions and present them with server-owned government provenance for up to the cache TTL.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Medium. The unauthenticated published Redis boundary and deserialization/consumption chain are explicit in repository code, but practical exploitability depends on whether a real deployment exposes that host port beyond a trusted developer machine and on crafting the exact GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer wire shape.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: At be68c887fe118d02d2bfac1987a0c51aa62abbb6, compose.yaml lines 35-42 publishes unauthenticated Redis; application.yml lines 35-46 configures no credential; CacheConfig lines 35-52 accepts com.mermaid polymorphic records; DrugPermissionApiClient lines 62-85 consumes Redis-cached government records; DrugService lines 338-361 turns them into medicine facts and server provenance.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable full-file static source-to-sink trace
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Validate the intended production network/Redis ACL configuration and, in an isolated local stack, write one serializer-compatible Permitted cache value to confirm a public drug request consumes it and labels it as government data.

source_record.closest_control: BasicPolymorphicTypeValidator restricts class names to application/JDK packages, which narrows gadget exposure but is not an authenticity boundary. Docker/network isolation may contain Redis in production, but no such production evidence is present and Compose publishes the port on the host.
source_record.impact: An actor with Redis network reachability can persist false product names, ingredients, licence state, prescription status, narratives, or warnings for attacker-chosen cache keys. The application can then use those values in allergy decisions and present them with server-owned government provenance for up to the cache TTL.
source_record.plausibility: Medium. The unauthenticated published Redis boundary and deserialization/consumption chain are explicit in repository code, but practical exploitability depends on whether a real deployment exposes that host port beyond a trusted developer machine and on crafting the exact GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer wire shape.
source_record.title: A reachable default Redis service can be used to poison trusted medicine caches
variant_titles: A reachable default Redis service can be used to poison trusted medicine caches
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Spring Cache trusts Redis bytes after only a polymorphic class-package check. That validator blocks arbitrary outside classes but intentionally permits the exact com.mermaid record classes an attacker needs to forge cached medicine facts; no authentication, MAC, or provenance binding protects values in the shown default deployment.

attacker_controlled_source: [worker-04:W04-S008-C04] Any process or adjacent-network actor able to reach the Compose-published Redis port can issue Redis write commands without repository-configured credentials. Internet reachability is deployment-dependent and not proven by this checkout.

evidence.boundary: Unauthenticated Redis command channel exposed by the default Compose port mapping, conditional on network reachability.
evidence.control: Polymorphic package allowlist; it controls class selection but not who wrote an allowed object or whether its medical fields came from a provider.
evidence.counterevidence: The repository labels Compose local-development infrastructure, and the threat model says production network controls are outside the repository. There is no public HTTP cache-write endpoint, and the package validator prevents arbitrary non-allowlisted class names; these facts lower reachability/confidence but do not close a reachable Redis deployment.
evidence.impact_path: Actor reaches Redis -> writes an expected cache key with an allowed application-record payload -> Spring cache deserializes it -> API client body is skipped -> DrugService trusts the forged record -> server emits false medicine facts with MFDS provenance.
evidence.preconditions: The deployed Redis endpoint is reachable by the actor, has no external ACL/TLS/auth override, and the attacker crafts the serializer's expected JSON/type metadata and cache key.
evidence.sink: GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer followed by @Cacheable medicine clients, DrugService allergy/provenance assembly, and public drug/chat responses.

affected_locations.detail: A matching schemaVersion returns parsed.data unchanged whenever the caller omits isValid; the TypeScript cast adds no runtime validation.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_parser

affected_locations.detail: loadChatSession supplies no validator, so {schemaVersion:'1.0',data:null} returns null instead of EMPTY_SESSION and remains in sessionStorage.
affected_locations.label: missing_chat_validator

affected_locations.detail: ChatProvider passes the null value to restoreSession, whose session.messages dereference throws before the provider or shell renders.
affected_locations.label: dereference_sink

affected_locations.detail: The root has no error boundary or recovery path, so the startup exception removes the full application safety surface.
affected_locations.label: missing_containment

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A browser-side attacker with one-time same-origin script execution, a malicious third-party script, extension/local-device access, or another Web Storage write primitive sets mermaid.chatSession.v2 to a valid-version envelope whose data is null.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Schema-version check only; no ChatSession shape validation or top-level null guard.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: tampered storage -> matching version accepted -> null returned -> restoreSession dereference -> uncaught root render exception -> application unavailable on reload.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The attacker needs a browser-side/local storage-write capability. This does not give an ordinary remote HTTP caller a new primitive; it makes a transient/browser-side compromise persist across reloads of the tab.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The effect is tab-scoped because sessionStorage dies with the tab, but it removes every safety surface and care-information path while the poisoned tab persists.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Synchronous ChatProvider initialization before the application shell and disclaimer render.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Write {"schemaVersion":"1.0","data":null} to sessionStorage key mermaid.chatSession.v2 and reload the same tab.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: JSON.parse returns {schemaVersion:'1.0',data:null}; read() lines 41-50 returns null because isValid is absent. loadChatSession passes the value to ChatProvider; restoreSession line 77 dereferences session.messages. main.tsx contains no error boundary. storage.test.ts covers stale JSON and saved-facility null data but not chat null data.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable parser-to-root static execution trace and target-test coverage inspection.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The old chat key is swept, transcripts stay in sessionStorage, restoreSession tolerates a non-array messages property, and read() catches JSON/version errors. None rejects null at the top level, and the failing dereference occurs outside read()'s try/catch.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Every reload of that tab throws before MobileShell, the emergency banner, navigation, and the mandatory disclaimer render, denying the user chat and care information until the session-storage key or tab is cleared.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The payload is a single valid JSON value and survives reloads in the same tab. schemaVersion matches, so read() neither removes it nor returns fallback; restoreSession immediately evaluates session.messages on null. Existing tests cover invalid JSON/version and nested malformed messages, but not null top-level chat data.

source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The versioned reader treats a compile-time generic cast as runtime validation and the chat loader does not supply a shape predicate before ChatProvider dereferences the returned value.

affected_locations.detail: read() validates JSON syntax and schemaVersion but returns parsed.data without checking that it exists or has the requested runtime shape.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_source

affected_locations.detail: loadChatSession returns that unchecked value from sessionStorage as ChatSession.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: The same-origin sessionStorage value mermaid.chatSession.v2, which the repository explicitly treats as untrusted because it can be stale, corrupted, or written by browser tooling/extensions, supplies a syntactically valid wrapper such as {schemaVersion:'1.0',data:null}.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted tab-scoped browser storage crosses into the globally mounted medical UI during application startup.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: The versioned reader is intended to reject corrupt/future data, and restoreSession is intended to prevent malformed stored conversations from blanking the app; neither validates the top-level ChatSession object.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: The failure is confined to that browser tab, sessionStorage is cleared when the tab closes, parse errors and version mismatches are removed, and malformed messages inside a non-null object are skipped. These limit persistence and attacker reach but do not make the top-level payload safe.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: The chat provider can fail during the root's initial render, leaving the user without the consultation UI, always-visible disclaimer, emergency presentation, map, or an in-app way to clear the bad session. Reloading the same tab repeats the failure until session storage is cleared or the tab is closed.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The tab's chat key must contain a matching-version wrapper with null/missing data, through storage corruption, browser tooling/extension access, or incompatible same-version code; normal current saveChatSession calls write a valid object.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: An uncaught null-property read during ChatProvider initialization aborts the root component path that owns all application screens and safety UI.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: The same-origin sessionStorage value mermaid.chatSession.v2, which the repository explicitly treats as untrusted because it can be stale, corrupted, or written by browser tooling/extensions, supplies a syntactically valid wrapper such as {schemaVersion:'1.0',data:null}.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: storage.ts:41-50 accepts a matching wrapper and returns parsed.data; loadChatSession at line 130 omits isValid. chatSession.tsx:158-159 invokes the loader and restoreSession during provider initialization, while restoreSession line 77 reads session.messages. A read-only Node check of JSON.parse('{"schemaVersion":"1.0","data":null}').data.messages produced TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'messages'). MobileShell.tsx:21 mounts ChatProvider above every screen and main.tsx:8-14 supplies no error boundary.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable inter-file static trace plus direct evaluation of the same-version null payload's JavaScript dereference.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Add a target-revision Vitest/browser case that seeds the exact sessionStorage value, mounts MobileShell, and asserts a safe empty session plus removal of the key. No target checkout or test artifact was created under this immutable worker contract.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: read() catches malformed JSON and removes mismatched versions, and restoreSession handles messages:null or malformed message entries after it has a non-null session object. Unlike loadSavedFacilities, loadChatSession passes no whole-object validator, so data:null and a missing data property bypass both controls; main.tsx adds no fallback boundary.
absorbed_provenance.impact: The chat provider can fail during the root's initial render, leaving the user without the consultation UI, always-visible disclaimer, emergency presentation, map, or an in-app way to clear the bad session. Reloading the same tab repeats the failure until session storage is cleared or the tab is closed.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Moderate and deterministic for the stated untrusted-storage boundary. The exact one-line same-version payload reaches the dereference; current application writes are well formed and a remote web attacker cannot set another origin's sessionStorage without an additional origin/extension capability, which limits reach but does not close stale/corrupt-state availability.

title: Same-version null chat-session data can blank the application at startup
vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control:
[worker-01:W01-A052-C01] The versioned reader treats a compile-time generic cast as runtime validation and the chat loader does not supply a shape predicate before ChatProvider dereferences the returned value.

[worker-04:W04-S053-C01] read() accepts parsed.data when the version matches and loadChatSession supplies no isValid predicate. ChatProvider then calls restoreSession(null), whose first guard dereferences session.messages before checking that messages is an array; the globally mounted tree has no recovery boundary.

affected_locations.detail: A version match returns data without shape validation when isValid is omitted.
affected_locations.label: untrusted_storage_parser

affected_locations.detail: loadPreferences accepts null, then synchronously dereferences stored.manualLocation; the same path is used by location resolution and preference updates.
affected_locations.label: missing_preferences_validator

affected_locations.detail: resolveLocation calls loadPreferences before it has returned a Promise, so the synchronous exception cannot become a rejection for the caller's .catch.
affected_locations.label: activation_path

affected_locations.detail: Opening Map invokes resolveLocation in an effect; no local try/catch or root error boundary contains a synchronous storage exception.
affected_locations.label: uncontained_effect_sink

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: A browser-side attacker, same-origin/third-party script, extension, or local-device actor writes a valid-version null envelope to the persistent mermaid.preferences.v1 localStorage key.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: Schema-version check and manualLocation migration, but no top-level Preferences shape check.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: persistent tampered preference -> version accepted -> null returned -> synchronous property dereference -> effect exception -> whole client safety/care UI unavailable.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: Browser-side/local storage-write capability is required; there is no ordinary remote HTTP-only path. localStorage makes the denial persist across tabs/restarts until storage is cleared.
candidate_local_attack_path.severity_facts: The attack removes the map/facility path precisely when nearby care is requested and can remove the always-visible disclaimer/navigation as collateral. The strong prerequisite materially limits severity.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Synchronous exception in MapScreen's passive effect before resolveLocation returns a Promise, with no application error boundary.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Write {"schemaVersion":"1.0","data":null} to localStorage key mermaid.preferences.v1, then activate Map.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: read() returns null for a matching-version preferences envelope. loadPreferences line 227 dereferences stored.manualLocation. resolveLocation executes that before constructing/returning its later Promise, while MapScreen attaches .catch only to the missing return value. main.tsx has no boundary and tests do not cover null top-level preferences data.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable storage-to-Map effect trace and exact target-test inspection.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: Allergy persistence is fail-closed when rememberAllergies is false, pre-DEV-206 objects are normalized with a manualLocation fallback, and saved facilities have a validator. No equivalent Preferences validator or synchronous exception containment exists.
absorbed_provenance.impact: The poisoned localStorage value survives new tabs and browser restarts. Activating Map throws outside its Promise rejection handler and, without an error boundary, can tear down the application instead of showing nearby pharmacies/hospitals or the persistent disclaimer.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: The one-line JSON payload deterministically survives read() and triggers stored.manualLocation on null. MapScreen catches only Promise rejections, but resolveLocation throws before returning a Promise. The persistent key is never removed because the exception occurs after read() accepts it.

source_record.title: A same-version null preference blob crashes the app when Map opens
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Preferences are treated as trusted after only a wrapper-version check; loadPreferences dereferences the unchecked top-level value synchronously before resolveLocation returns its Promise.

worker_local_reconciliation.note: Unique worker-local instance retained after semantic reconciliation.

absorbed_provenance.title: A same-version null preference blob crashes the app when Map opens
absorbed_provenance.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Preferences are treated as trusted after only a wrapper-version check; loadPreferences dereferences the unchecked top-level value synchronously before resolveLocation returns its Promise.

absorbed_provenance.attacker_controlled_source: The persistent same-origin localStorage value mermaid.preferences.v1, an untrusted/stale browser value, supplies a valid version wrapper whose data is null or absent.

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Persistent untrusted browser preferences cross into the location and nearby-facility UI when the user activates Map.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Versioned storage and the Map error path are intended to turn bad local/location state into a safe fallback instead of crashing; the missing Preferences shape validator leaves a synchronous gap before the Promise handler.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: The Chat tab initially remains usable until Map activation, current writes are valid, malformed JSON and version mismatches self-heal, and the user can recover by clearing localStorage. The persistent same-version shape nevertheless bypasses every in-app cleanup path.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Opening Map can raise an uncaught client exception that removes access to nearby-care results and can take down the shared shell, including chat and safety messaging. Because the poison value is in localStorage, reloads and new tabs continue to fail on Map until the key is manually cleared or overwritten.
candidate_local_attack_path.preconditions: The persistent preferences key must contain matching-version null/missing data and the user must activate Map. Normal current savePreferences writes a valid value; the source is corruption, stale/incompatible code, or same-origin/extension/device modification.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: The null-property read escapes the Map activation effect with no application error boundary, denying the nearby-care screen and potentially the entire shared shell.
candidate_local_attack_path.source: The persistent same-origin localStorage value mermaid.preferences.v1, an untrusted/stale browser value, supplies a valid version wrapper whose data is null or absent.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: storage.ts:224-229 reads preferences without isValid and accesses stored.manualLocation. facilities.ts:82-90 calls loadPreferences synchronously, and MapScreen.tsx:94-104 calls resolveLocation() before the .then/.catch chain exists. A read-only Node check of the matching-version wrapper produced TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'manualLocation'). main.tsx:8-14 has no component-level recovery wrapper.
candidate_local_validation.method: Immutable source-to-effect trace plus direct evaluation of the accepted null payload's JavaScript dereference.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Add a target-revision browser/Vitest case that seeds localStorage with the exact wrapper, activates Map, and verifies fallback preferences plus key cleanup without losing the shared shell. No mutable checkout execution was used for this immutable shard.

absorbed_provenance.closest_control: The reader rejects invalid JSON and schema versions, current writes strip non-consented allergies, and the Map effect attaches a catch for asynchronous location failures. None checks the top-level Preferences shape, and the catch is installed only after resolveLocation returns; saved facilities demonstrate that the reader supports an exact isValid predicate when one is supplied.
absorbed_provenance.impact: Opening Map can raise an uncaught client exception that removes access to nearby-care results and can take down the shared shell, including chat and safety messaging. Because the poison value is in localStorage, reloads and new tabs continue to fail on Map until the key is manually cleared or overwritten.
absorbed_provenance.plausibility: Moderate and deterministic for corrupt/stale local state. The target's tests explicitly call stored blobs untrusted, but current savePreferences emits a valid object and a remote attacker needs an additional same-origin, extension, device, or incompatible-build path to place the null value.

source_record.title: A same-version null preferences blob can tear down the application when Map opens
variant_titles: A same-version null preferences blob can tear down the application when Map opens
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: loadPreferences calls read() without a Preferences validator and immediately evaluates stored.manualLocation. resolveLocation invokes that loader synchronously; MapScreen's promise catch cannot handle an exception thrown before resolveLocation returns a Promise, and the application root provides no recovery boundary.

title: Null model product identity crashes pre-validator grounding

source_record.title: Malformed nested model fields can trigger a deterministic server error
source_record.source_or_privileged_boundary: Structured LLM response fields such as `productNameKo`.
source_record.closest_control: Provider-output parser/fallback boundary.
source_record.sink_or_broken_control: HTTP 500 and occupied request resources.
source_record.impact: A malformed or adversarial provider response can make chat requests fail reliably, degrading availability.

candidate_local_validation.method: Static exception-path construction from accepted fallback shape to null-sensitive validator operation.
candidate_local_validation.evidence: A nonnull drug element with null `productNameKo` survives fallback and reaches `groundedDrugs.get(null)`.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: The reviewed implementation is in nested immutable revision be68c887fe118d02d2bfac1987a0c51aa62abbb6; the .claude/worktrees/interesting-borg-847dc7 tree is absent from outer target 654f906e00e81648d1482210b6a9171747dddd75, so current deployment reachability must be confirmed. Confirm the exact exception and HTTP mapping with a stub provider returning the malformed object.

source_record.title: Schema-less model null product name crashes before validation
source_record.source_or_privileged_boundary: User-influenced schema-less/retry model output with a drug object whose productNameKo is null.
source_record.closest_control: Fallback rejects top-level null elements and validator would reject unknown products, but both miss ordering/nested null.
source_record.sink_or_broken_control: Immutable Map.get(null) throws NPE during grounding before the validator can reject an unknown product.
source_record.impact: A malformed model response turns a fail-closed refusal into an HTTP 500; repeated prompting/provider faults reduce chat availability.

source_record.source: LLM JSON returned after structured output is unsupported or rejected

source_record.sink: groundAllergyChecks calls an immutable map's get() with drug.productNameKo() before AnswerValidator
source_record.impact: A malformed or prompt-injected provider response can turn an anonymous chat request into an unhandled 500 and consume the expensive upstream work first
source_record.plausibility: Schema enforcement is optional/retried away; Jackson records admit null strings; fallback checks only top-level collection nulls; grounding runs before validation
source_record.validation_recommended: Use a schema-less fake provider response with one drug whose productNameKo is null and assert a safe local fallback, not INTERNAL_ERROR

title: Explicit null urgency suppresses fail-upward emergency handling

affected_locations.detail: Unknown urgency strings map upward to URGENT, but an explicit JSON null deserializes to null without invoking the enum creator.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Null-element checks omit urgency.level and withGuarantees replaces only a wholly null urgency object.
affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper

affected_locations.detail: Pre-model triage is finite and its TODO identifies severe allergic reaction, seizure, poisoning/overdose, and head injury as uncovered cases.
affected_locations.label: closest_control

affected_locations.detail: The call-action invariant runs only when level equals EMERGENCY, so null incurs no violation.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Client normalization keeps a non-null urgency object even if its level member is null.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Emergency presentation is gated on level exactly equaling emergency, so null suppresses the banner.
affected_locations.label: sink

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Untrusted provider response for a public chat request that passed the finite EmergencyTriage matcher.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: EmergencyTriage prevents this for its enumerated phrases and unknown non-null strings map to URGENT, but neither covers explicit null on emergencies outside the finite rule set.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: The triage source itself documents uncovered emergency categories.; The bypass is deterministic once a null response is supplied, though obtaining it through prompt injection is model-dependent.; A global disclaimer still says to call 119 in an emergency, which limits but does not replace symptom-specific escalation.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: The request reaches the model because the rule matcher has no hit. -> Jackson materializes Urgency with level null without invoking the enum's unknown-value fallback. -> Fallback normalization retains that object and the validator does not require an emergency action. -> The accepted response crosses to the frontend, whose normalization also retains the object. -> ChatScreen evaluates null === emergency as false and omits the emergency banner.
preconditions: The user's emergency wording is not one of EmergencyTriage's current regex matches.
The provider returns schema-valid-enough JSON with a nonempty summary, a non-null urgency object, and level set to null.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: frontend/src/components/ChatScreen.tsx:140-151
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Untrusted provider response for a public chat request that passed the finite EmergencyTriage matcher.

candidate_local_validation.counterevidence: The server's rule-based triage returns a server-authored emergency answer before the model for its current red-flag phrases.; A response-format schema and system prompt make null less likely from a compliant provider, and the main summary may still mention emergency care.
candidate_local_validation.evidence: An isolated ObjectMapper harness using an equivalent @JsonCreator enum deserialized {level:null} to a record whose level was null, confirming that the fail-upward creator is bypassed for JSON null.; Fallback line 96 preserves any non-null urgency object; AnswerValidator lines 103-107 does nothing unless the level is exactly EMERGENCY.; The frontend preserves the nested object and ChatScreen's strict equality check treats null as non-emergency.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static trace plus isolated Jackson ObjectMapper reproduction of the enum-null behavior.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: A controller-level regression test using the target's exact dependency graph and a mocked upstream response with urgency.level null was not added or run.

source_record.closest_control: EmergencyTriage prevents this for its enumerated phrases and unknown non-null strings map to URGENT, but neither covers explicit null on emergencies outside the finite rule set.
source_record.dedupe_notes: Keep separate from C0002: this is a concrete structural null/defaulting bug with an emergency-banner sink, not the broader absence of semantic output validation.
source_record.impact: For an emergency outside the rule-based matcher, a null model urgency level reaches the browser as non-emergency and suppresses the emergency banner and action enforcement.
source_record.plausibility: Jackson deserializes JSON null for a reference-typed enum as null without calling the @JsonCreator, and every downstream condition tests exact EMERGENCY rather than rejecting/defaulting null.
source_record.sink_or_broken_control: JSON null bypasses Urgency.Level.from's fail-upward mapping; StructuredOutputFallback preserves the partially populated object and AnswerValidator's emergency-action check treats null as non-emergency.
source_record.source_or_privileged_boundary: The untrusted LLM/provider controls the nested urgency object after any request that does not match the server's finite pre-triage patterns.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: A malformed or compromised government API response supplies a valid item sequence but omits ITEM_NAME; a public caller can select the affected product via chat search.

receipt.attacker: Compromised/malicious upstream provider or actor able to induce a malformed selected record
receipt.outcome: Repeatable request failures and bounded cache-poisoned availability loss
receipt.preconditions: Valid itemSeq and guidance record, missing/null ITEM_NAME, and chat retrieval selects the product
receipt.severity_facts: External compromise/malformed-data precondition; deterministic crash; cache extends the effect; no code execution or cross-user data exposure.
receipt.sink: Uncaught NullPointerException in chat request and six-hour cached detail value
receipt.transformations: toDetail -> Drug.nameKo null -> grounded HashMap null key -> Map.copyOf

receipt.attacker: Compromised/malformed upstream plus unauthenticated query caller
receipt.entry_point: Ingredient search used by chat retrieval
receipt.outcome: Repeatable chat/drug retrieval denial for affected keys
receipt.preconditions: At least two returned rows reach the name tie-breaker and one has null ITEM_NAME
receipt.severity_facts: External-content precondition; deterministic crash; six-hour cache window; no code execution
receipt.sink: Uncaught NullPointerException in retrieval; malformed list retained in cache
receipt.transformations: JSON text(null) -> Permitted -> Drug summary -> BY_USEFULNESS sort

receipt.counterevidence: Requires malformed/compromised upstream content and usually affects queries selecting that product rather than all requests; ordinary provider data supplies ITEM_NAME.
receipt.evidence: PublicApiResponse.text can return null, PermittedDetail/Drug impose no non-null constructor invariant, HashMap accepts null, and JDK Map.copyOf rejects it. No intervening check or catch exists.
receipt.method: Static malformed-row construction and exact exception-path reasoning

receipt.counterevidence: Requires malformed/compromised upstream content and at least two rows whose earlier comparator keys tie; normal provider rows include names.
receipt.evidence: toPermitted can construct nameKo=null; summary preserves it; the comparator's final natural-order String comparison rejects null. A two-row tied response reaches it and @Cacheable persists the source list.

source_record.closest_control: Rows without itemSeq are filtered, missing narrative becomes Narrative.EMPTY, per-product detail assembly catches exceptions thrown inside DrugService, and upstream response sizes are bounded.
source_record.control_gap: Name presence is not validated at the external boundary and the later immutable-map conversion introduces an uncaught null constraint after DrugService's catch scope.
source_record.impact: One malformed cached product can make every chat request selecting it return an internal error for the cache window, and a compromised upstream can target many products to degrade chat availability.
source_record.plausibility: Moderate. Normal government rows include names, but the threat model explicitly requires hostile/malformed upstream tolerance and the failure is deterministic once the shape occurs.
source_record.sink_control_evidence: The only groundability rejection checks ingredient normalization, not product identity. The controller catches LLM failures after retrieval but does not contain this exception.
source_record.source_evidence: toDetail passes PublicApiResponse.text(row, ITEM_NAME) directly into PermittedDetail with no required-field check. DrugService copies it into Drug.nameKo.
source_record.title: Malformed upstream drug names can crash public retrieval paths
source_record.validation_recommended: With fake clients, return PermittedDetail(itemSeq valid, nameKo null) and a present narrative, then assert current retrieve throws; validate fail-closed row rejection and no malformed cache entry.
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: DrugContextRetriever builds a mutable map that accepts the null key, then Map.copyOf throws NullPointerException during every affected context assembly.

title: Every provider HTTP 400 triggers a second schema-less LLM request

affected_locations.detail: The retry predicate checks only status 400 and assumes response_format caused it.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: post() performs each authenticated provider request.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: The retry removes only response_format, retaining any attacker-driven cause such as oversized/invalid conversation input.
affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation

candidate_local_attack_path.boundary: Unauthenticated user input influencing a structured-output provider request
candidate_local_attack_path.control: The retry is limited to one and only applies for configured structured-output models. That caps amplification at roughly 2x but does not distinguish attacker-induced 400s.
candidate_local_attack_path.counterevidence: Public route; One retry maximum; Long provider timeouts and cost-bearing calls
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: Submit input that the provider rejects with 400. -> The proxy misclassifies the status as schema incompatibility. -> The proxy sends the request again without the schema.
preconditions: Configured model is on the structured-output allowlist.
User input can elicit a provider 400 for a reason other than response_format.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Second authenticated LLM POST
candidate_local_attack_path.source: Unauthenticated user input influencing a structured-output provider request

candidate_local_validation.counterevidence: Non-400 errors are not retried.; At most one fallback retry is coded.
candidate_local_validation.evidence: Lines 70-80 route all WebClientResponseException 400 responses into a second post.; withoutSchema removes only one field at lines 96-99.
candidate_local_validation.method: Static branch and retry-sink review
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Use a configured provider or mock to confirm a user-triggerable non-schema 400 reaches the retry.; Confirm whether rejected requests are billed by the deployed provider.

source_record.closest_control: The retry is limited to one and only applies for configured structured-output models. That caps amplification at roughly 2x but does not distinguish attacker-induced 400s.
source_record.dedupe_notes: Independent retry-control instance; do not merge into generic no-rate-limit unless validation shows no distinct second call.
source_record.impact: An attacker-triggered bad request can double outbound attempts, connection work, latency, and potentially provider accounting, compounding public-endpoint exhaustion.
source_record.plausibility: The predicate is exactly status==BAD_REQUEST; it does not inspect a provider error code or prove response_format was the failing field.
source_record.sink_or_broken_control: When structured output is enabled, every 400 is classified as schema rejection and post() is invoked again with the same request minus response_format.
source_record.source_or_privileged_boundary: The caller controls conversation content and size that can make a provider reject a request with HTTP 400.

closest_control: MAX_OPEN_NOW_CANDIDATES truncates the distance-sorted candidate list before availability is evaluated.

title: One failed hospital detail lookup discards every otherwise valid nearby hospital

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: Parallel.map returns only after all detail mappings and propagates a mapper exception.

affected_locations.detail: toHospital calls findByYkiho without a per-row catch/degradation path.

affected_locations.label: entrypoint/wrapper
affected_locations.detail: Unlike pharmacy timetable lookup, hospital detail lookup has no per-row recovery.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: Any mapped exception terminates collectList and the request.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: A malformed/failing HIRA detail response for any one returned ykiho controls the mapper exception.
source_record.source_evidence: Static per-row exception trace demonstrates single-input amplification to whole-response failure.
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Hospital fan-out is fail-fast; unlike pharmacy, no row-level exception handling retains verified directory data with unknown hours.
source_record.sink_control_evidence: Static per-row exception trace demonstrates single-input amplification to whole-response failure.

source_record.control_gap: Hospital fan-out is fail-fast; unlike pharmacy, no row-level exception handling retains verified directory data with unknown hours.
source_record.impact: One bad detail record turns all nearby hospitals into a 503, hiding valid care locations during an upstream partial failure.
source_record.plausibility: Parallel.map's collected Mono propagates any inner error; toHospital does not catch PublicApiException.

title: Geocoding accepts non-finite and out-of-range coordinates as successful results

source_record.title: Non-finite or out-of-range upstream geocodes are returned as map coordinates

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: parseDouble accepts `NaN` and infinities and no latitude/longitude range or finite check follows.

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The values are serialized to the browser as a successful geocode result.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: The external Naver JSON controls address.x/y strings on the threat-model boundary.
source_record.source_evidence: Exact external numeric field -> unvalidated GeocodeResult trace.
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: Parsing validates only that Java accepts a double; it does not require finite longitude [-180,180] or latitude [-90,90].
source_record.sink_control_evidence: Exact external numeric field -> unvalidated GeocodeResult trace.
source_record.closest_control: Fixed provider/TLS and a status/array shape check reduce likelihood; NumberFormatException becomes a safe unavailable response for nonnumeric text only.
source_record.control_gap: Parsing validates only that Java accepts a double; it does not require finite longitude [-180,180] or latitude [-90,90].
source_record.impact: Malformed/compromised data can break map positioning or route a sick user to an invalid location while the response is marked successful.
source_record.plausibility: `Double.parseDouble("NaN")` and infinities succeed, and ordinary out-of-range numbers also survive.

candidate_local_validation.method: worker-local consolidation of independently recorded immutable-source validation receipts

receipt.evidence: `NaN`, `Infinity`, latitude 999 and longitude -999 pass parseDouble and are added without a bounds branch.

candidate_local_attack_path.method: worker-local consolidation of source, closest-control, sink, impact, precondition, and counterevidence paths

receipt.impact_path: Malformed numeric token -> accepted double -> browser map consumes invalid coordinate -> location guidance fails/misplaces
receipt.preconditions: Upstream/intermediary returns a non-finite or out-of-range coordinate.
receipt.counterevidence: Normal Naver responses are expected valid; client map libraries may reject rather than misplace invalid values.

title: A composite drug card attributes e약은요 and DUR facts to the permission dataset

source_record.title: Composite drug cards attribute e약은요 and DUR facts to the permission dataset

affected_locations.label: root_control
affected_locations.detail: The service joins permission, easy-drug narrative, and four DUR warning results into one Drug but assigns only source(itemSeq).

affected_locations.label: sink
affected_locations.detail: The sole SourceRef title names only MFDS drug product licence information.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: The Drug record explicitly contains facts from three separate datasets but has a single source field.

affected_locations.label: concrete_implementation
affected_locations.detail: DUR safety warnings are fetched from distinct operation families and lose operation-level provenance at the join.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: MFDS permission, e약은요, and DUR responses are separate external records with distinct retrieval/failure histories.
source_record.source_evidence: The immutable aggregate shape contains three factual sources and exactly one SourceRef titled `drug product licence information`; no later code can reconstruct e약은요/DUR provenance.
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: DrugService collapses all three into one card carrying a SourceRef whose title and record semantics describe only the permission dataset; narrative and contraindication facts have no exact source record or retrieval provenance.
source_record.sink_control_evidence: The immutable aggregate shape contains three factual sources and exactly one SourceRef titled `drug product licence information`; no later code can reconstruct e약은요/DUR provenance.
source_record.closest_control: The provider is the same ministry and the itemSeq join is intended, which preserves agency-level attribution. It does not preserve dataset/operation origin, time, or fallback status required for exact server-owned provenance.
source_record.control_gap: DrugService collapses all three into one card carrying a SourceRef whose title and record semantics describe only the permission dataset; narrative and contraindication facts have no exact source record or retrieval provenance.
source_record.impact: Users and downstream validation can treat official dosing/narrative/contraindication text as verified by the wrong dataset and cannot tell which upstream actually supplied or failed for a safety-critical fact.
source_record.plausibility: Lines 137-150 fetch three services; lines 152-164 construct one Drug with narrative and warnings; line 164 supplies only the permission source created at 355-362. The domain has no per-fact source slots.

candidate_local_validation.method: worker-local consolidation of independently recorded immutable-source validation receipts

receipt.method: Immutable static branch and dataflow trace at the pinned revision
receipt.evidence: Static record-shape and constructor inspection proves narrative and durWarnings share the permission SourceRef and that no other source object is retained.

receipt.closest_control: Single SourceRef at aggregate level rather than per contributing record/fact
receipt.sink_or_broken_control: Verified drug card, chat context, and source footer
receipt.impact_path: distinct upstream facts -> joined Drug -> only permission SourceRef retained -> all facts displayed under that attribution
receipt.preconditions: A detail/card includes nonempty narrative or DUR warnings.
receipt.counterevidence: All services are operated by MFDS and use the same ITEM_SEQ namespace, so agency identity is not fabricated; the missing distinction is dataset, retrieval time, and fallback origin.

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: Unauthenticated search and detail callers control a repeated or comma-separated `exclude_ingredients` Set, including the count and length of its elements.

receipt.control: No application-level cardinality or term-length validation.
receipt.counterevidence: Finite URI limits and government result caps bound a single request; empirical cost may be too low to reach a security threshold.
receipt.impact: CPU/memory pressure and degraded availability for legitimate medicine lookups.

receipt.method: Immutable complexity analysis across request binding, normalization, and product allergy checks.
receipt.proof_gap: Measure the largest exclusion set accepted by the deployed ingress/Tomcat configuration and benchmark concurrent worst-case searches to establish exploitability and saturation threshold.

source_record.closest_control: Tomcat/proxy URI-size limits and upstream result caps indirectly bound one request, but their configured effective limits are not established by this code and no rate limit prevents repetition.
source_record.control_gap: Unlike query/ingredient, the collection and elements have no Bean Validation constraint. Every element is normalized, then AllergyChecker compares each retained key against every ingredient of each returned product.
source_record.impact: Oversized allergen sets can consume CPU and allocation per public request and amplify upstream-backed searches into application-layer denial of service.
source_record.plausibility: The loops are explicit and there is no code-level cap or truncation; search results apply the check repeatedly across products.

title: Unparseable hospital lunch hours are treated as no closure

affected_locations.detail: Any lunchWeek text outside one strict tilde pattern becomes Optional.empty.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Optional.empty bypasses lunch closure and allows an OPEN result from broad weekday hours.
affected_locations.label: sink

attack_path.counterevidence: The captured fixture uses the accepted '12:30 ~ 13:30' format; HIRA may standardize this field.
attack_path.impact: A user can be directed to a hospital that is closed for lunch.
attack_path.prerequisites: A real closure expressed outside the accepted regex and a request during that interval.
attack_path.severity_facts: Impact is a bounded care-location integrity error with a provider-format prerequisite.
attack_path.source: The external HIRA detail API supplies a real lunch closure in a variant or malformed textual format.
attack_path.steps: HIRA returns variant text; parser yields empty; weekday hours say open; lunch check is skipped; OPEN is returned.

source_record.attacker_source: The external HIRA detail API supplies a real lunch closure in a variant or malformed textual format.
source_record.broken_control: Optional.empty conflates no lunch break with an unparseable lunch break.
source_record.closest_control: The regex safely rejects ambiguous values and the card advises calling, but rejection is not propagated as unknown.

canonical_comparison.rationale: R01-CAN-054 describes the same defect only in .claude/worktrees/interesting-borg-847dc7 paths. Its canonical remediation boundary is explicitly nested-only and says to keep an outer analogue separate unless the same deployed instance/control is proven. The frozen candidate is the outer deployed path backend/src/main/... at the immutable target; fixing the absent nested copy would not fix it. R01-CAN-018's generic semantic-prose remediation also does not necessarily overwrite this server-owned field.

source_record.attacker_precondition: Hybrid/live mode returns cacheable values and the attacker varies valid keys during the six-hour window.

source_record.attack_path: vary valid request key each call -> cold upstream lookup -> distinct six-hour Redis entry -> repeat until Redis/host memory fails
source_record.attack_scenario: A client varies the least-significant digits of valid latitude/longitude values and rotates valid-length drug queries/itemSeq values. Every cold request creates new six-hour cache namespaces, including large hospital batches. Redis grows until host/container memory is exhausted or writes fail under its default unbounded/noeviction behavior.
source_record.description: Redis entries live for six hours, while no maxmemory, eviction policy, or container memory limit is configured. Hospital list keys embed exact client-controlled double coordinates and radius, and drug caches use raw names, ingredients, and identifiers. An attacker can manufacture effectively unbounded distinct keys; hospital values may each contain up to 2,000 rows.
source_record.impact: Redis or host out-of-memory, cache write failures, loss of availability, and amplified upstream traffic after cache failure.
source_record.recommendation: Canonicalize/quantize location keys without returning incorrect distance facts, validate and normalize all identifier/search keys, cap cached row counts and cache cardinality, configure Redis maxmemory plus an intentional eviction policy, and set a container memory limit with alerts.
source_record.title: Attacker-controlled cache keys can grow Redis without a memory bound

validation.method: cache-key inventory and configuration negative search
validation.observation: Multiple @Cacheable keys are direct request values; the 6h TTL is the only lifecycle bound and neither Spring nor Compose configures Redis memory/eviction limits.

attacker_controlled_source: An anonymous caller chooses arbitrary itemSeq values on the public drug-detail route.

candidate_local_attack_path.source: An anonymous caller chooses arbitrary itemSeq values on the public drug-detail route.
candidate_local_attack_path.control: Positive permissionDetail and easyDrugBySeq values live six hours with no route admission, cache cardinality, Redis maxmemory, or eviction budget.
candidate_local_attack_path.sink: Distinct itemSeq values create Redis entries in both positive detail caches; HYBRID fallback can make the positive keyspace query-independent.
candidate_local_attack_path.impact_path: An anonymous caller chooses arbitrary itemSeq values on the public drug-detail route. -> Positive permissionDetail and easyDrugBySeq values live six hours with no route admission, cache cardinality, Redis maxmemory, or eviction budget. -> Distinct itemSeq values create Redis entries in both positive detail caches; HYBRID fallback can make the positive keyspace query-independent. -> Enumeration or fallback-driven misses consume Redis memory, evict useful state, and degrade availability.

candidate_local_validation.evidence: Independent Round-04 semantic reconciliation retained 3 source member(s) as R04-NEW-005; counterevidence remains for centralized validation.

TTL bounds lifetime, not aggregate cardinality; HYBRID fixture fallback broadens positive-entry reachability.

closest_control: Positive permissionDetail and easyDrugBySeq values live six hours with no route admission, cache cardinality, Redis maxmemory, or eviction budget.
impact: Enumeration or fallback-driven misses consume Redis memory, evict useful state, and degrade availability.

remediation_subsumption.rationale: R01-CAN-087 covers one-to-six detail fan-out and empty DUR behavior; R01-CAN-086/R01-CAN-088 cover other key families. None necessarily bounds these positive itemSeq caches.
remediation_subsumption.remediation_boundary: Validate/admit itemSeq at the route, share a bounded positive-detail cache policy, and configure Redis maxmemory/eviction.
counterevidence: Live valid identifiers are finite and successful entries require a response.

title: Chat open-now flow discards unknown-hours facilities

affected_locations.detail: The model's openNow flag is forwarded directly to fetchFacilities.
affected_locations.label: unsafe filter request

affected_locations.detail: open_now filters out all non-true operations, including unknown.
affected_locations.label: pharmacy omission

affected_locations.detail: The same filter removes unknown hospitals.
affected_locations.label: hospital omission

affected_locations.detail: Only returned facilities are shown; an empty list becomes a no-known-open sentence with no call option.
affected_locations.label: user-visible sink

affected_locations.detail: The shared helper documents that backend filtering drops unknown hours and that Chat has not adopted the split.
affected_locations.label: negative control

affected_locations.detail: MapScreen fetches unfiltered data, separates unknown hours, and preserves a call-to-confirm list.
affected_locations.label: correct comparison

attack_path: Source: model-authored OPEN_FACILITY_MAP action with openNow=true.
Transform: NearbyFacilities forwards the flag to the server.
Control failure: FacilityService removes every null operation.
Sink: chat map and accessible list receive no unknown facility or phone number.
Impact: a potentially open care option disappears during a time-sensitive search.
source_record.counterevidence: The empty-state wording says that no facility is known to be open and does not literally label unknown as closed. The safety defect is availability: the unknown option and phone number are removed exactly where the Map screen preserves them.
source_record.description: NearbyFacilities forwards the model's openNow flag to the backend. The backend intentionally filters to Boolean.TRUE, so facilities with isOpenNow=null never reach the chat map or its accessible list. The dedicated Map screen already avoids this by fetching unfiltered data, splitting unknown hours, and showing a call-to-confirm list; facilitySplit explicitly says the Chat tab has not adopted the protection.
source_record.exploit_scenario: A chat answer opens an open-now pharmacy map when no schedule-known pharmacy is open but one nearby pharmacy has null hours. The server removes the unknown facility, and the chat says no pharmacies were found instead of showing its phone number for confirmation.
source_record.impact: A sick user can be denied the only nearby care option because missing schedule data is treated as grounds for disappearance. This is the omission form of the null-is-not-closed invariant.
preconditions: A schema-valid assistant UI action requests OPEN_FACILITY_MAP with openNow=true.
A nearby pharmacy or hospital has unknown hours and may in fact be open.
source_record.remediation: Make NearbyFacilities fetch openNow=false, split results client-side when the action asks for open now, put known-open facilities on the map, and render unknown facilities in an accessible Hours unknown - call to confirm list with telephone links, matching MapScreen. Preserve full-set fixture provenance and add an inline-chat regression test.
source_record.title: Chat open-now maps discard every unknown-hours facility instead of preserving call-to-confirm options

source_record.attack_path_facts: Malformed upstream coordinates + small distance -> accepted Facility -> SDK marker at invalid/wrong position or map-level marker error.
source_record.broken_control: Pharmacy checks only non-null coordinates; hospital checks only finiteness. Neither enforces latitude [-90,90] and longitude [-180,180] at the public-upstream boundary.
source_record.candidate_local_validation: Static predicate proof confirms finite out-of-range values survive both parsers; FacilityMap forwards them directly to new naver.maps.LatLng.
source_record.closest_control: The request controller has explicit coordinate bounds, but equivalent response-boundary validation is absent.
source_record.impact: A facility can be plotted at an invalid or wrong place, or marker construction can fail and remove the map's usable care-navigation surface.
source_record.plausibility: A finite latitude of 999 and a distance of 0 pass the current checks and radius predicate. Request-side coordinate validation does not validate provider response coordinates.
source_record.remediation_requirement: Validate provider coordinates for finiteness and WGS84 range before any distance or rendering use; drop the row as malformed source data.
source_record.sink: A row with an accepted distance passes filtering and reaches Naver LatLng/marker rendering.
source_record.source: A malformed or compromised NMC/HIRA row with finite but out-of-range latitude or longitude and a plausible distance.

title: Malformed successful public-API envelopes become empty success

affected_locations.detail: Only resultCode is required and totalCount defaults to zero.
affected_locations.label: root_control

affected_locations.detail: Unexpected row shapes silently become an empty list.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

affected_locations.detail: The shared empty list becomes a successful pharmacy result.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Malformed pages can be treated as empty or truncated success.
affected_locations.label: sink

affected_locations.detail: Malformed drug payloads become empty Optional/list results.
affected_locations.label: sink

source_record.attack_path_facts: Malformed successful envelope -> shared parser empty list -> adapter empty collection -> UI states no matching care/drug data.
source_record.broken_control: requireOk verifies only the header code. items silently returns an empty list for every shape other than object/array, and totalCount silently defaults to zero.
source_record.candidate_local_validation: Static constructed-envelope evaluation proves the malformed-success path reaches an empty list. The behavior is distinct from legitimate totalCount=0 with absent items.
source_record.closest_control: An error code enum for SOURCE_PAYLOAD_INVALID exists, but this shared parser never emits it for inconsistent success bodies.
source_record.impact: Schema drift or a compromised upstream can turn source failure into 'no pharmacies/hospitals/medicines found', an unsafe factual absence rather than an explicit unavailable/unknown state.
source_record.plausibility: For body.totalCount=1 and body.items='malformed', requireOk passes, items returns [], and no adapter raises SOURCE_PAYLOAD_INVALID.
source_record.remediation_requirement: Validate the success-body schema and consistency (especially totalCount versus rows); raise a non-empty payload-invalid error for impossible shapes while preserving legitimate zero-result envelopes.
source_record.sink: All facility and drug adapters consume the empty list as a successful no-results answer.
source_record.source: A government gateway response with resultCode 00 but inconsistent body metadata or an unexpected items shape.
source_record.title: Successful but malformed public-API bodies fail open as empty results

title: Fallback overnight inference reuses today as yesterday

affected_locations.detail: The location-row start/end semantics are documented as unconfirmed.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: Raw fallback times carry no day identity.
affected_locations.label: source

affected_locations.detail: carriesInto explicitly requires yesterday's interval.
affected_locations.label: closest_control

affected_locations.detail: The same fallback interval is used as today and yesterday.
affected_locations.label: broken_control

source_record.attack_path_facts: No weekly table -> wrapping fallback row -> early-morning carriesInto on same-day object -> inferred-open marker/filter result.
source_record.broken_control: The fallback creates only one interval from the row and calls both containsStartingToday and carriesInto on that same interval, although OpenInterval documents that carriesInto must be asked of yesterday's interval.
source_record.candidate_local_validation: Code-path validation is complete and the logic mismatch is explicit; live upstream semantics require confirmation before final severity assignment.
source_record.closest_control: WeeklyHours correctly obtains yesterday's distinct row before carriesInto; the fallback does not have such evidence and should degrade to unknown.
source_record.impact: A pharmacy may be shown as open based on ambiguous/today-only data, directing a user to unavailable care.
source_record.plausibility: The upstream startTime/endTime semantics are explicitly unconfirmed. If they mean today's window, the branch is wrong; if they mean a typical daily interval, the result may be intended. That semantic uncertainty is the remaining proof gap.
source_record.remediation_requirement: Do not infer the carryover half without prior-day evidence; either fetch/retain yesterday's row or return unknown for ambiguous early-morning fallback intervals.
source_record.sink: The operation can be inferred open at 01:00 from a 21:00-02:00 row without evidence that yesterday's shift existed.
source_record.source: A pharmacy lacking weekly detail whose location row has a wrapping start/end interval, queried in the early morning.
source_record.title: Fallback overnight inference asks today's interval about yesterday's carryover

source_record.attacker_controlled_source: The sensitive value is a victim-entered address; the relevant recipients and possible log readers are the application infrastructure and Naver provider.

candidate_local_attack_path.control: length validation and safe local logging, but no disclosed/minimized provider handoff

evidence: FacilityMap passes the trimmed raw address unchanged to fetchGeocode.
Both facilities.ts lines 42-49 and GeocodeClient lines 47-52 encode it as a GET query parameter.
candidate_local_validation.proof_gap: Review application/Naver retention and access-log redaction, and verify the shipped UI privacy disclosure before final severity.

source_record.closest_control: GeocodeController trims, rejects blank values, and caps length at 200; GeocodeClient deliberately avoids logging WebClient exceptions because they may include the address, but the UI gives no provider-sharing or retention disclosure.
source_record.counterevidence: The user knowingly initiates address search, geocoding inherently requires revealing the query to a provider, HTTPS protects transport, query length is bounded, and the reviewed Java client never includes the query in its own log messages.
source_record.impact: A precise home/work/care address can be retained in access logs or provider telemetry and linked to a health-service session despite the user only intending to choose a map centre.
source_record.title: A typed street address crosses two GET query-string and third-party retention boundaries without privacy copy
source_record.vulnerable_sink_or_broken_control: The address appears in both application and provider request URLs before results are returned.
source_record.why_plausible: The code itself recognizes the query as a person's location and suppresses exception logging, establishing sensitivity; URL placement and provider disclosure remain deterministic.

source_record.closest_control: The parser bounds shape and size and matching uses a normalized form, but output copy keeps the original text and has no delimiter or safe-display encoding for medical semantics.
